[title] INTERSTELLAR

[author_marker] written by
[author_marker] Jonathan Nolan and Christopher Nolan

[author_names] Transferred to PDF from:
[author_names] "Interstellar - The Complete
[author_names] Screenplay with Selected
[author_names] Storyboards"
[author_names] Published November 2014 by
[author_names] Faber & Faber Ltd. (UK)

[author_names] FOR EDUCATIONAL PURPOSES ONLY

[action] BLACK. THE GENTLE SOUND OF WIND IN CORN

[action] A row of books. From spaces between them, dust falls.

[] I
[] N
[] T
[] E
[] R
[centered] S
[] T
[] E
[] L
[] L
[] A
[] R

[character] ELDERLY FEMALE VOICE
[parenthetical] (V.O.)
[dialogue] Sure. Dad was a farmer.

[action] BRIGHT CORN STALKS FILL THE FRAME, SWAYING IN THE BREEZE

[character] ELDERLY FEMALE VOICE
[parenthetical] (V.O.)
[dialogue] Like everybody else back then.

[action] The wind is RISING, shaking the plants more FORCEFULLY ...

[action] Insert cut: a WOMAN in her eighties against a dark
[action] background.

[character] ELDERLY WOMAN
[dialogue] Course, he didn’t start that way...

[action] The WIND IS HOWLING, SHRIEKING and we RIP INTO -

[scene_heading] EXT. THE STRATOSPHERE - DAY

[action] BURNING through the fringes of space -

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT - CONTINUOUS

[action] A young PILOT fights his BUFFETING craft -

[character] RADIO
[parenthetical] (O.S.)
[dialogue] Computer says you’re too tight -

[character] PILOT
[dialogue] I got this -

[action] The Pilot grabs a panicked glance at his instruments -

[character] RADIO
[parenthetical] (O.S.)
[dialogue] Crossing the Straights ... shutting
[dialogue] it down, Cooper. Shutting it all
[dialogue] down ...

[page_number] 2.

[character] PILOT
[dialogue] NO! I need the power up -

[scene_heading] EXT. THE STRATOSPHERE - CONTINUOUS

[action] The BLACK and RED SKY starts SPINNING HORRIFYINGLY -

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT - DAY

[action] As the controls RIP themselves free, the pilot SHOUTS and we
[action] -

[transition] CUT TO:

[scene_heading] INT. BEDROOM, FARMHOUSE - NIGHT

[action] A man WAKES, nightmare SWEATY. This is COOPER.

[centered] YOUNG GIRL’S VOICE
[parenthetical] (O.S.)
[] Dad? Dad?

[action] Cooper turns: in the doorway - his sleepy ten-year-old
[action] daughter. This is MURPH.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Sorry. Go back to sleep.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] I thought you were the ghost.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] There’s no ghost, Murph.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Grandpa says you can get ghosts.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Maybe Grandpa’s a little too close
[dialogue] to being one himself. Back to
[dialogue] sleep.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Were you dreaming about the crash?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Back to sleep, Murph.

[action] Murph shuffles back out the door. Cooper moves to the
[action] window. DAWN breaks over an ENDLESS SEA OF CORN ...

[page_number] 3.

[character] ELDERLY FEMALE VOICE
[parenthetical] (V.O.)
[dialogue] Corn, sure. But dust. In your ears,
[dialogue] your mouth...

[action] INSERT CUT: AN OLD-TIMER IN CLOSE UP, WATERY-EYED, DESCRIBES
[action] DUST BOWL CONDITIONS.

[character] OLD-TIMER
[parenthetical] (V.O.)
[dialogue] Dust just everywhere. Everywhere.

[scene_heading] EXT. COOPER’S FARM - MORNING

[action] An old man, handkerchief across his face, sweeps dust out of
[action] the door onto the porch. This is Grandpa (DONALD).

[scene_heading] INT. KITCHEN, FARMHOUSE - MORNING

[action] Cooper pours himself coffee as Donald puts grits on the
[action] table. TOM, Cooper’s fifteen-year-old son, stuffs his face.

[action] Murph, wet hair, towel around neck, plays with pieces of a
[action] MODEL (a lunar lander).

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] Not at the table, Murph.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Dad, can you fix this?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] (takes pieces, frowning)
[dialogue] What’d you do to my lander?

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Wasn’t me.

[character] TOM
[dialogue] Lemme guess - your ghost?

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] It knocked it off my shelf. It
[dialogue] keeps knocking books off.

[character] TOM
[dialogue] There’s no such things as ghosts,
[dialogue] dumb-ass -

[page_number] 4.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (to Tom)
[dialogue] Hey -

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] I looked it up, it’s called a
[dialogue] poltergeist.

[character] TOM
[dialogue] Dad, tell her.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Murph, you know that’s not
[dialogue] scientific.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] You say science is about admitting
[dialogue] what we don’t know.

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] She’s got you there.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (hands her pieces)
[dialogue] Start looking after our stuff.

[action] Donald looks at Cooper, admonishing. Cooper shrugs.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Fine. Murph, you wanna talk
[dialogue] science, don’t just tell me you’re
[dialogue] scared of some ghost - record the
[dialogue] facts, analyze, present your
[dialogue] conclusions.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Sure.

[action] Cooper gets up, grabs his keys.

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] Hold up.
[parenthetical] (Off look.)
[dialogue] Parent-teacher conferences.
[dialogue] ’Parent’ - not ’grandparent’.

[scene_heading] EXT. FARMHOUSE - MOMENTS LATER

[action] The kids pile into an old pickup truck, scraping DUST off
[action] the seats. Cooper, coffee in hand, peers at a black cloud.

[page_number] 5.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Dust storm?

[character] DONALD
[parenthetical] (shakes his head)
[dialogue] Nelson’s torching his whole crop.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Blight?

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] They’re saying it’s the last
[dialogue] harvest for okra. Ever.

[action] Cooper stares at the smoke. Uneasy. Gets into the truck.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Shoulda planted corn like the rest
[dialogue] of us.

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] Be nice to Miss Hanley. She’s
[dialogue] single.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] What’s that supposed to mean?

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] Repopulating the Earth - start
[dialogue] pulling your weight.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Start minding your business.

[scene_heading] INT./EXT. PICKUP TRUCK ON DIRT ROAD - MOMENTS LATER

[action] Cooper sips his coffee, steering while Murph shifts -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Okay, gimme second -

[action] Murph wrestles the long gear stick into second. Cooper sips.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Now third -

[action] Murph struggles to find third - GRIND.

[character] TOM
[dialogue] Find a gear, dumb-ass.

[page_number] 6.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Shut up, Tom!

[action] BANG - A TIRE BLOWS OUT. Cooper stops the truck.

[character] TOM
[dialogue] What’d you do, Murph?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] She didn’t do anything. We lost a
[dialogue] tire is all.

[character] TOM
[dialogue] Murphy’s Law.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Shut up, Tom.

[action] Cooper gets out of the truck, checks the flat, turns to Tom.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Grab the spare.

[character] TOM
[dialogue] That is the spare.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Okay, patch kit.

[character] TOM
[dialogue] How’m I supposed to patch it out
[dialogue] here?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Figure it out. I’m not always going
[dialogue] to be here to help you.

[action] Tom moves to the back of the truck. Murph is there.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Why’d you and Mom name me after
[dialogue] something bad?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] We didn’t.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Murphy’s Law?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Murphy’s Law doesn’t mean bad stuff
[dialogue] will happen. It means ’whatever can
[dialogue] happen, will happen’. And that
[dialogue] sounded just fine to us.

[page_number] 7.

[action] Murph frowns, hearing something ...

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] What?

[action] Then Cooper hears it, too. A LOW RUMBLE. Cooper GRABS Murph
[action] as a DRONE SOARS low overhead -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Come on!

[action] Cooper jumps into the truck - he pulls out a laptop and
[action] antenna hands them to Murph - shouts at Tom -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Get in!

[character] TOM
[parenthetical] (jack in hand)
[dialogue] What about the tire?

[scene_heading] INT./EXT. PICKUP TRUCK THROUGH FIELDS - MOMENTS LATER

[action] Close on the SHREDDING TIRE as the truck BARRELS through
[action] cornfields. Murph fires up the laptop. Cooper strains to see
[action] through the cornstalks, scanning the horizon -

[character] TOM
[dialogue] There!

[action] To the right, the dark shape of the drone, cruising low over
[action] the fields. Cooper JERKS the wheel - the drone has long,
[action] thin wings like a U-2, but no cockpit.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Indian air force surveillance
[dialogue] drone. Solar cells could power an
[dialogue] entire farm.
[parenthetical] (To Tom.)
[dialogue] Take the wheel -

[action] Tom takes the wheel - Cooper hands Murph the antenna.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Keep it pointed right at it -

[action] Cooper works the laptop - the screen fills with Hindi.
[] Faster, Tom. I’m losing it.

[action] Tom WEAVES through the corn - they round a corner, almost
[action] HIT a HARVESTER - BANG - the truck loses a wing mirror -

[action] Ahead the drone SOARS, banking, pulling away -

[page_number] 8.

[action] The truck BURSTS out of the corn, Cooper’s nose is in the
[action] laptop.

[character] TOM
[dialogue] Dad?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Almost got it. Don’t stop.

[action] In front of them, the drone plummets from view into the next
[action] valley - the path ahead leads to a three-hundred-foot drop.

[character] TOM
[dialogue] DAD ...

[action] Cooper looks up.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Tom!

[action] Tom locks up the brakes. Cooper looks at him - he shrugs.

[character] TOM
[dialogue] You told me to keep going.

[action] Cooper grabs the laptop and opens the door.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Guess that answers the ’if I told
[dialogue] you to drive off a cliff’ scenario.

[action] Murph is still pointing the antenna.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] We lost it.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (smiles)
[dialogue] No, we didn’t.

[action] The DRONE SOARS BACK OVER THEM - Cooper is moving his
[action] fingers across the track pad, PILOTING THE DRONE.

[action] As the kids watch, Cooper sends the drone soaring over them,
[action] banking above the valley. Cooper crouches next to Murph.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Want to give it a whirl?

[action] Murph, guided by Cooper, moves her fingers across the track
[action] pad - the massive drone BANKS in response. Murph is in
[action] heaven.

[page_number] 9.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Let’s set her down next to the
[dialogue] river.

[scene_heading] EXT. RIVERBANK - MOMENTS LATER

[action] The truck limps up to the drone. Cooper and the kids climb
[action] down. Cooper runs a hand along the smooth carbon flank of
[action] the aircraft.

[character] TOM
[dialogue] How long you think it’s been up
[dialogue] there?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Delhi mission control went down
[dialogue] same as ours, ten years ago.

[character] TOM
[dialogue] It’s been up there ten years? Why’d
[dialogue] it come down so low?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Sun finally cooked its brain. Or it
[dialogue] came down looking for something.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] What?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Some kind of signal. Who knows?

[action] Cooper finds an access hatch. Pries it open. Examines the
[action] black-box brain of the machine.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] What are you going to do with it?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Give it something socially
[dialogue] responsible to do, like drive a
[dialogue] combine.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Couldn’t we just let it go? It’s
[dialogue] not hurting anyone.

[action] Cooper looks down at his daughter. Good kid.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] This thing has to adapt, just like
[dialogue] the rest of us.

[page_number] 10.

[scene_heading] EXT. COUNTY SCHOOL - DAY

[action] The truck pulls up to school, drone fuselage hanging out.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] How’s this work? You guys come
[dialogue] with?

[character] TOM
[dialogue] I’ve got class. But she ...

[action] Pats Murph on shoulder.
[] Needs to wait.

[action] Murph glares at Tom as he hops out.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Why? What?

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Dad, I had a thing ... well,
[dialogue] they’ll tell you about it. Just try
[dialogue] and ...

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Am I gonna be mad?

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Not with me. Just try not to -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Relax, I got this.

[action] Murph pulls out a notebook. Starts drawing a BARCODE.

[scene_heading] INT. PRINCIPAL’S OFFICE - LATER (MORNING)

[action] Cooper enters, awkward. The PRINCIPAL (male, fifties) turns
[action] from the window.

[character] PRINCIPAL
[dialogue] Little late, Coop.
[parenthetical] (Indicates chair.)
[dialogue] Guess you had to stop off at the
[dialogue] Asian fighter plane store.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (sits, smiles)
[dialogue] Actually, sir, it’s a surveillance
[dialogue] drone. With outstanding solar
[dialogue] cells.

[page_number] 11.

[action] Cooper nods at Murph’s teacher, Ms Hanley, thirties,
[action] attractive.

[character] PRINCIPAL
[dialogue] We got Tom’s scores back. He’s
[dialogue] going to make an excellent farmer.
[dialogue] Congratulations.

[action] The Principal slides a paper across the desk.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (taken aback)
[dialogue] What about college?

[centered] PRINCIPAL
[dialogue] The university only takes a
[dialogue] handful. They don’t have resources
[dialogue] -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I’m still paying taxes - where’s
[dialogue] that go? There’s no more armies.

[character] PRINCIPAL
[dialogue] Not to the university. Coop, you
[dialogue] have to be realistic.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You’re ruling him out for college
[dialogue] now? He’s fifteen.

[character] PRINCIPAL
[dialogue] Tom’s score simply isn’t high
[dialogue] enough.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] What’re you? About a
[dialogue] thirty-six-inch waist?
[parenthetical] (Beat.)
[dialogue] Thirty-inch inseam?

[character] PRINCIPAL
[dialogue] I’m not sure I see what -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You’re telling me you need two
[dialogue] numbers to measure your own ass,
[dialogue] but just one to measure my son’s
[dialogue] future?

[action] Ms Hanley stifles a laugh. The Principal shoots her a look.

[page_number] 12.

[character] PRINCIPAL
[dialogue] You’re a well educated man, Coop. A
[dialogue] trained pilot -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] And an engineer.

[character] PRINCIPAL
[dialogue] Okay. Well, right now the world
[dialogue] doesn’t need more engineers. We
[dialogue] didn’t run out of planes, or
[dialogue] television sets. We ran out of
[dialogue] food .

[action] Cooper leans back. He’s not going to win this one.

[character] PRINCIPAL
[dialogue] The world needs farmers. Good
[dialogue] farmers, like you. And Tom.
[parenthetical] (Smiles benignly.)
[dialogue] We’re a caretaker generation. And
[dialogue] things are getting better. Maybe
[dialogue] your grandchildren -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Are we done, sir?

[character] PRINCIPAL
[dialogue] No. Ms Hanley is here to talk about
[dialogue] Murph.

[action] Cooper shifts his gaze to Ms Hanley.

[character] MS HANLEY
[dialogue] Murph’s a bright kid. A wonderful
[dialogue] kid, Mr Cooper. But she’s been
[dialogue] having a little trouble ...

[action] Ms Hanley places a textbook on the desk.
[] She brought this to school, to show
[] the other kids the section on the
[] lunar landings ...

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Yeah, it’s one of my old textbooks,
[dialogue] she likes the pictures.

[character] MS HANLEY
[dialogue] This is an old federal textbook.
[dialogue] We’ve replaced them with corrected
[dialogue] versions.

[page_number] 13.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Corrected?

[character] MS HANLEY
[dialogue] Explaining how the Apollo missions
[dialogue] were faked to bankrupt the Soviet
[dialogue] Union.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You don’t believe we went to the
[dialogue] moon?

[character] MS HANLEY
[parenthetical] (tolerant smile)
[dialogue] I believe it was a brilliant piece
[dialogue] of propaganda. The Soviets
[dialogue] bankrupted themselves pouring
[dialogue] resources into rockets and other
[dialogue] useless machines.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] ’Useless machines’?

[character] MS HANLEY
[dialogue] Yes, Mr Cooper. And if we don’t
[dialogue] want a repeat of the wastefulness
[dialogue] and excess of the twentieth
[dialogue] century, our children need to learn
[dialogue] about  this  planet, not tales of
[dialogue] leaving it.

[action] Cooper considers this in silence. Looks at Ms Hanley.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] One of those useless machines they
[dialogue] used to make was called an MRI. And
[dialogue] if we had any of them left, the
[dialogue] doctors might have been able to
[dialogue] find the cyst in my wife’s brain
[dialogue] before she died, rather than
[dialogue] afterwards. Then she could be
[dialogue] sitting here listening to this,
[dialogue] which’d be good, cos she was always
[dialogue] the  calmer  one ...

[action] Ms Hanley looks at Cooper, embarrassed. Then -

[character] MS HANLEY
[dialogue] I’m sorry about your wife, Mr
[dialogue] Cooper. But Murph got into a fist
[dialogue] fight with several of her
[dialogue] classmates over this Apollo
[dialogue] nonsense and we thought it best to
[dialogue] (MORE)

[page_number] 14.

[character] MS HANLEY (cont’d)
[dialogue] bring you in and see what ideas you
[dialogue] might have for dealing with her
[dialogue] behavior on the home front.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Sure. Well, there’s a ball game
[dialogue] tomorrow night, and Murph’s going
[dialogue] through a bit of a baseball phase.
[dialogue] There’ll be candy and soda ...

[action] Ms Hanley looks at him, expectant.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I think I’ll take her to that.

[action] Ms Hanley turns to the Principal, not happy.

[scene_heading] EXT. PICKUP TRUCK OUTSIDE SCHOOL - MOMENTS LATER

[action] Murph looks up from her notebook at her dad, expectantly.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] How’d it go?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I, uh ... got you suspended.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] What?!

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Sorry.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Dad! I told you not to -

[action] The CB radio CRACKLES to life.

[character] CB OPERATOR
[dialogue] Cooper? Boots for Cooper.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Cooper.

[character] BOOTS
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] Coop, those combines you rebuilt
[dialogue] went haywire.

[page_number] 15.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Power the controllers down for a
[dialogue] couple minutes.

[character] BOOTS
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] Did that. You should come take a
[dialogue] look, it’s kinda weird.

[scene_heading] EXT. FARMHOUSE - DAY

[action] Cooper and Murph pass a slow-moving harvester pulling up to
[action] the house, which is surrounded by AUTOMATED FARM MACHINES.
[action] They’ve nosed up to the house like animals at a Nativity.
[action] Boots, the farm hand, approaches -

[character] BOOTS
[dialogue] One by one they been peeling off
[dialogue] from the fields and heading over.

[action] Cooper pops open the cabin to a harvester. Checks the auto
[action] pilot hooked up to the controls.

[character] BOOTS
[dialogue] Something’s interfering with their
[dialogue] compass ...

[action] Cooper jumps down and heads to the front door. Enters.

[character] BOOTS (O.S.)
[dialogue] Magnetism or some such ...

[scene_heading] INT. FARMHOUSE - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper looks at the kitchen. Nothing. Murph comes in.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] What is it, Dad?

[action] A small BANG from upstairs. Cooper heads up.

[scene_heading] INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS

[action] Murph’s bedroom clearly used to be her mother’s. Cooper, in
[action] the doorway, looks at the wall of BOOKS opposite - several
[action] GAPS. He looks down at some books on the floor.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Nothing special about which books.
[parenthetical] (Off look.)
[dialogue] (MORE)

[page_number] 16.

[character] MURPH (cont’d)
[dialogue] Been working on it, like you said.

[action] Murph holds up her notebook with its barcode.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] I counted the spaces.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Why?

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] In case the ghost’s trying to say
[dialogue] something. I’m trying Morse.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Morse?

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Yeah. Dots and dashes, used for -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Murph, I know what Morse code is. I
[dialogue] just don’t think your bookshelf’s
[dialogue] trying to talk to you.

[action] He leaves. Murph, embarrassed, turns back to the shelf.

[scene_heading] INT. FRONT PORCH, FARMHOUSE - NIGHT

[action] Donald hands Cooper a beer.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Had to reset every compass clock
[dialogue] and GPS to offset for the anomaly.

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] Which is?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] No idea. If the house was built on
[dialogue] magnetic ore, we’d’ve seen this the
[dialogue] first time we switched on a
[dialogue] tractor.

[action] Donald nods. Sips.

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] Sounds like your meeting at school
[dialogue] didn’t go so well.

[page_number] 17.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (sighs)
[dialogue] We’ve forgotten who we are, Donald.
[dialogue] Explorers, pioneers. Not
[dialogue] caretakers.

[action] Donald nods, thoughtful. Weighs up his words.

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] When I was a kid it felt like they
[dialogue] made something new every day. Some
[dialogue] gadget or idea. Like every day was
[dialogue] Christmas. But six billion people
[dialogue] ... just try to imagine that. And
[dialogue] every last one of them trying to
[dialogue] have it all.

[action] He turns to Cooper.
[] This world isn’t so bad. And Tom’ll
[] do just fine - you’re the one who
[] doesn’t belong. Born forty years
[] too late, or forty years too early.
[] My daughter knew it, God bless her.
[] And your kids know it. ’Specially
[] Murph.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] We used to look up and wonder at
[dialogue] our place in the stars. Now we just
[dialogue] look down and worry about our place
[dialogue] in the dirt.

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] Cooper, you were good at something
[dialogue] and you never got a chance to do
[dialogue] anything with it. I’m sorry. But
[dialogue] that’s not your kids’ fault.

[action] Cooper looks up at the stars above.

[character] OLD-TIMER (V.O.)
[dialogue] May 14th. Never forget. Clear as a
[dialogue] bell. You’d never think ...

[action] INSERT CUT: THE OLD-TIMER REMEMBERS. CUT TO A SECOND
[action] OLD-TIMER ...

[character] SECOND OLD-TIMER
[dialogue] When the first of the real big ones
[dialogue] rolled in ... I thought it was the
[dialogue] end of the world.

[page_number] 18.

[scene_heading] EXT. BASEBALL FIELD - LATE AFTERNOON

[action] The CRACK of ball off bat - a pop-fly caught to a trickle of
[action] applause. Half-filled stands at what looks like a minor
[action] league game.

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] In my day we had real ball players.
[dialogue] Who’re these bums?

[action] As the team runs in from the field we see: NEW YORK YANKEES.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Well, in my day people were too
[dialogue] busy fighting over food for
[dialogue] baseball, so consider this
[dialogue] progress.

[action] Murph offers Donald some popcorn.

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] Fine. But popcorn at a ball game is
[dialogue] unnatural. I want a hot dog.

[character] MURPH
[parenthetical] (confused)
[dialogue] What’s a hot dog?

[action] Cooper sits with Tom a row in front.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] The school says you’re gonna follow
[dialogue] in my footsteps. I think that’s
[dialogue] great.

[character] TOM
[dialogue] You think that’s great?

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] You hate farming, Dad. Grandpa
[dialogue] said.

[action] Cooper looks at Donald, who shrugs ’sorry’.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] What’s important is how you feel
[dialogue] about it, Tom.

[character] TOM
[dialogue] I like what you do. I like our
[dialogue] farm.

[page_number] 19.

[action] On the field: the batter hits one along the ground - it
[action] rolls to an infielder’s foot - but the infielder IGNORES it,
[action] STARING up at the sky. The crowd starts to look up ...

[character] OLD-TIMER (V.O.)
[dialogue] You’ve never seen the like. Black.
[dialogue] Just black ...

[action] INSERT CUT: THE OLD-TIMER CHOKES BACK FEAR AS HE REMEMBERS.

[action] Cooper stares at the horizon, where an ENORMOUS BLACK DUST
[action] STORM IS MASSING. People start leaving, tying handkerchiefs
[action] across their faces.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Come on, guys.

[scene_heading] INT./EXT. PICKUP TRUCK - MOMENTS LATER

[action] Cooper speeds along as Donald and the kids stuff RAGS into
[action] cracks and vents ... behind them the WALL OF BLACK DUST
[action] ADVANCES, SWALLOWING UP ROADS, BUILDINGS. A nasty SOUND is
[action] developing - the truck ROCKING with GUSTS of wind ...

[action] Suddenly, BLACK DUST ENVELOPS the car, LIGHTNING CRACKLING.

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] It’s a bad one ...

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Mask up, guys.

[action] Murph and Tom take SURGICAL MASKS out of the glove box.

[scene_heading] EXT. FARMHOUSE - CONTINUOUS

[action] VISIBILITY MERE FEET as the dust storm BRUTALIZES the farm.
[action] The truck CRAWLS up to the house. Cooper leans in to try and
[action] see better ... CRACK - a panel of sheet metal SMASHES into
[action] the windshield - Cooper turns - wrestles Murph out of the
[action] truck as Donald blindly stumbles towards the front door with
[action] Tom ...

[scene_heading] INT. FARMHOUSE - CONTINUOUS

[action] The SHUTTERS BANG as the wind WHIPS around the house,
[action] FORCING JETS OF DUST up through cracks in the window frames,
[action] floorboards ... Donald SLAMS the door. Murph is COUGHING ...
[action] Cooper looks around. Sees dust coming from upstairs.

[page_number] 20.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Did you both shut your windows?

[action] Tom nods. Murph looks at Cooper. Runs for the stairs.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Wait -

[scene_heading] INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper gets to the doorway. Murph stands in the middle of
[action] the room, STARING. Cooper SHUTS the window. The dust hangs
[action] in the relative quiet. Murph is staring, TRANSFIXED, at
[action] LINES where dust is FALLING UNNATURALLY FAST, STREAMING DOWN
[action] through the air, collecting on the floor in a PATTERN -

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] The Ghost.

[action] Cooper STARES at dust collecting like snow on power lines...

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Grab your pillow, sleep in with
[dialogue] Tom.

[scene_heading] EXT. FARMHOUSE - DAWN - MORNING

[action] Calm. Dust settled.

[scene_heading] INT. TOM’S ROOM - CONTINUOUS

[action] Murph slips out of bed, wrapped in her blanket. Pads down
[action] the hall, peeks in her bedroom door at -

[scene_heading] INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper sits, staring at the PATTERN of dust: thick radial
[action] lines, like a CIRCULAR BARCODE. Murph sits down next to her
[action] dad. They STARE at it together. He holds up a coin ...

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] It’s not a ghost ...

[action] Cooper tosses the coin across a line. It SHOOTS at the floor
[action] -
[] It’s gravity.

[page_number] 21.

[scene_heading] INT. SAME - LATER

[action] Donald pokes his head in.

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] I’m dropping Tom, then heading to
[dialogue] town ...
[dialogue] (Looks at dust pattern.)
[dialogue] You wanna clean that up when you’ve
[dialogue] finished praying to it?

[action] Cooper reaches for Murph’s notebook. Starts writing ...

[scene_heading] INT. KITCHEN, FARMHOUSE - LATER

[action] Murph fills a glass of water. Picks up a plate of
[action] sandwiches.

[scene_heading] INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS

[action] Follow Murph into the room, to find Cooper standing there.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I got something.

[action] Cooper is pointing to the thick and thin radial lines -
[] Binary. Thick is one, thin is zero
[] - it’s numbers ... number pairs...

[action] He holds up the notebook to show Murph the number pairs.
[] Coordinates.

[scene_heading] INT. KITCHEN, FARMHOUSE - MOMENTS LATER

[action] Cooper and Murph pore over MAPS. Cooper TOSSES one aside,
[action] lays it out on the table. Finds a spot. Looks up at Murph
[action] ...

[scene_heading] EXT. FARMHOUSE - MOMENTS LATER

[action] Cooper packing his truck - sleeping bag, flashlight ...

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] You can’t leave me behind!

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Grandpa’s back in two hours.

[page_number] 22.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] You don’t know what you’re going to
[dialogue] find -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] That’s why I can’t take you.

[scene_heading] INT. KITCHEN, FARMHOUSE - MOMENTS LATER

[action] Cooper grabs the maps and a bottle of water. He calls up -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Murph?

[action] Nothing.
[] Murph, just wait here for Grandpa.
[] Tell him I’ll call him on the
[] radio.

[scene_heading] INT./EXT. PICKUP TRUCK ON ROAD - MOMENTS LATER

[action] Cooper drives, map spread on the wheel, looks for a pen -
[action] reaches over to the passenger wheel well - lifts a BLANKET -
[action] Murph is there -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] JESUS!

[action] The truck WOBBLES as Cooper regains control.
[] Murph, what are you doing?!

[action] Murph is LAUGHING as she climbs into the passenger seat -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] It’s not funny -

[action] But Murph’s laugh is infectious.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] You wouldn’t be here if it wasn’t
[dialogue] for me.

[action] Cooper hands Murph the map.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Fair enough. Make yourself
[dialogue] useful...

[action] The pickup cruises down the road, heading for the MOUNTAINS.

[page_number] 23.

[scene_heading] EXT. PLAINS APPROACHING MOUNTAINS - DUSK

[action] The tiny pickup is dwarfed by the darkening foothills.

[scene_heading] EXT. DIRT ROAD OFF MOUNTAIN PASS - NIGHT

[action] Cooper pulls up to a gate in chain-link fence. Murph is
[action] asleep next to him.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Murph. Murph.

[action] Murph wakes.
[] I think this is as far as we get.

[action] Murph glances out at the fence. Closes her eyes again.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Why? You didn’t bring the
[dialogue] bolt-cutters?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I like your spirit, young lady.

[action] Cooper gets out of the truck, retrieves his bolt-cutters and
[action] comes up to the fence. He looks up and down the road.
[action] Nothing. He reaches out and puts the jaws of the cutters -

[action] WHAM! SPOTLIGHTS IN COOPER’S EYES - A HARSH ELECTRIC VOICE -

[character] VOICE
[dialogue] STEP AWAY FROM THE FENCE.

[action] Cooper drops the cutters, puts his hands in the air -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Don’t shoot! My child is in the
[dialogue] car! I’m unarmed! My daughter is -

[action] Murph watches, terrified as, with a ZAP, Cooper DROPS. Murph
[action] SCRAMBLES back along the seat as MASSIVE FOOTSTEPS APPROACH.
[action] The door is WRENCHED open - a BLINDING LIGHT

[character] VOICE
[dialogue] DON’T BE AFRAID.

[action] Murph SCREAMS.

[page_number] 24.

[scene_heading] INT. BRIGHT, INDUSTRIAL ROOM - LATER

[action] Cooper comes to, sitting in a chair. Opposite him is an
[action] ARTICULATED MACHINE. A VOICE emanates from its side.

[character] MACHINE
[dialogue] How did you find this place?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Where’s my daughter?

[character] MACHINE
[dialogue] You had the coordinates for this
[dialogue] facility marked on your map. Where
[dialogue] did you get them?

[action] Cooper leans in to the machine.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] WHERE’S MY DAUGHTER?!

[action] Cooper’s scream REVERBERATES. Cooper sizes up the machine.
[] You might think you’re still in the
[] Marines, but the Marines don’t
[] exist anymore, pal. I’ve got grunts
[] like you mowing my grass ...

[action] The Machine RISES to its full height.

[character] MACHINE
[dialogue] How did you find us?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] But you don’t look like a lawnmower
[dialogue] to me ... you, I’m gonna turn into
[dialogue] an overqualified vacuum cleaner -

[character] FEMALE VOICE (O.S.)
[dialogue] No, you’re not.

[action] Cooper turns to see a businesslike woman in her thirties.

[character] WOMAN
[dialogue] Tars, back down, please.

[action] The machine, TARS, sinks back down.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You’re taking a risk using
[dialogue] ex-military for security. They’re
[dialogue] old, their control units are
[dialogue] unpredictable ...

[page_number] 25.

[character] WOMAN
[dialogue] Well, that’s what the government
[dialogue] could spare.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Who are you?

[character] WOMAN
[dialogue] Dr Brand.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I knew a Dr Brand once. But he was
[dialogue] a professor -

[character] WOMAN (BRAND)
[dialogue] What makes you think I’m not?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] And nowhere near as cute.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] You think you can flirt your way
[dialogue] out of this mess?

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (honest, scared)
[dialogue] Dr Brand, I have no idea what this
[dialogue] mess is. I’m scared for my little
[dialogue] girl and I want her by my side.
[dialogue] Then I’ll tell you anything you
[dialogue] want to know. Okay?

[action] Brand considers this. Turns to Tars.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Get the principals and the girl
[dialogue] into the conference room.
[parenthetical] (To Cooper.)
[dialogue] Your daughter’s fine. Bright kid.
[parenthetical] (Rises.)
[dialogue] Must have a very smart mother.

[scene_heading] INT. UNDERGROUND FACILITY - MOMENTS LATER

[action] Cooper follows Brand into a corridor. Tars LURCHES behind.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] It’s pretty clear you don’t want
[dialogue] visitors - why not let us back up
[dialogue] from your fence and be on our way?

[page_number] 26.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] It’s not that simple.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Sure it is. I don’t know anything
[dialogue] about you or this place.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Yes, you do.

[action] Brand ushers Cooper through a door into a conference room -

[scene_heading] INT. CONFERENCE ROOM - CONTINUOUS

[action] Where an OLD MAN is crouched down, talking to Murph.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Dad!

[action] Murph runs into Cooper’s arms. The Old Man SMILES at Cooper.

[character] OLD MAN
[dialogue] Hello, Cooper.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (stunned)
[dialogue] Professor Brand?

[character] MAN AT TABLE (DOYLE)
[dialogue] Just take a seat, Mr Cooper.

[action] Cooper and Murph sit at a table where five people are
[action] waiting - a bespectacled man, WILLIAMS, leans forward to
[action] address Cooper.

[character] WILLIAMS
[dialogue] Explain how you found this
[dialogue] facility.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Stumbled across it. Looking for
[dialogue] salvage and I saw the fence -

[character] WILLIAMS
[dialogue] You’re sitting in the world’s best
[dialogue] kept secret - you don’t stumble in.
[dialogue] And you certainly don’t stumble
[dialogue] out.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] Cooper, please. Cooperate with
[dialogue] these people.

[page_number] 27.

[action] Cooper looks nervously around the room.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] It’s hard to explain, but we
[dialogue] learned these coordinates from an
[dialogue] anomaly ...

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] What sort of anomaly?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I don’t want to term it
[dialogue] ’supernatural’ ... but ...

[action] Cooper is losing them. Williams leans forward. Serious.

[character] WILLIAMS
[dialogue] You’re going to have to, Mr Cooper.
[dialogue] Real quick.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] After that last storm, it was a
[dialogue] pattern ... in dust ...

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] It was gravity.

[action] All eyes turn to Murph. She’s said the magic word. Doyle
[action] looks at Professor Brand, excited. Turns to Cooper -

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] Where was this gravitational
[dialogue] anomaly?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Look, I’m happy you’re excited
[dialogue] about gravity, but if you want more
[dialogue] answers from us I’m gonna need
[dialogue] assurances -

[character] WILLIAMS
[dialogue] Assurances?

[action] Cooper looks at Murph. Then covers Murph’s ears.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] That we’re getting out of here ...
[dialogue] and not in the trunk of some car.

[action] Brand laughs. Williams smiles. Cooper looks confused.

[page_number] 28.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] Don’t you know who we are, Coop?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] No. No, I don’t.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] (points around table)
[dialogue] Williams, Doyle, Jenkins, Smith,
[dialogue] you already know my father,
[dialogue] Professor Brand. We’re  NASA .

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] NASA?

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] NASA. Same NASA you flew for.

[action] Now Cooper is laughing, too. Murph looks around, confused.

[scene_heading] INT. UNDERGROUND FACILITY - MOMENTS LATER

[action] Professor Brand shows Cooper the facility.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I heard you got shut down for
[dialogue] refusing to drop bombs from the
[dialogue] stratosphere onto starving people.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] When they realized killing other
[dialogue] people wasn’t a long term solution
[dialogue] they needed us back. Set us up in
[dialogue] the old NORAD facility. In secret.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Why secret?

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] Public opinion won’t allow spending
[dialogue] on space exploration. Not when
[dialogue] we’re struggling to put food on the
[dialogue] table.

[action] Professor Brand ushers Cooper through a large door -

[page_number] 29.

[scene_heading] INT. GREENHOUSE - CONTINUOUS

[action] Professor Brand gestures to large PLANTATIONS under glass.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] Blight. Wheat seven years ago, okra
[dialogue] this year. Now there’s just corn.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] But we’re growing more than ever -

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] Like the potatoes in Ireland, like
[dialogue] the wheat in the dust bowl, the
[dialogue] corn will die. Soon.

[action] Brand enters with Murph. She shows her the greenhouses.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] We’ll find a way, we always have.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] Driven by the unshakable faith that
[dialogue] the Earth is ours.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Not just ours, but it is our home.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] Earth’s atmosphere is 80 percent
[dialogue] nitrogen. We don’t even breathe
[dialogue] nitrogen.

[action] Professor Brand shows him a blighted stalk.
[] Blight does. And as it thrives our
[] air contains less and less oxygen
[] ...

[action] Professor Brand gestures over at Murph ...
[] The last people to starve will be
[] the first to suffocate. Your
[] daughter’s generation will be the
[] last to survive on Earth.

[action] Cooper looks over at Murph. Then back to Professor Brand.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Tell me this is where you explain
[dialogue] how you’re going to save the world.

[page_number] 30.

[scene_heading] INT. VAST CIRCULAR CHAMBER (LAUNCH FACILITY) - MOMENTS LATER

[action] Cooper and Professor Brand enter like ants in a grain silo.
[action] A ROCKET is on a pad, DWARFED by the circular chamber. Far
[action] above, a ring of mirrors reflects the dawn down into the
[action] facility.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] We’re not meant to save the world
[dialogue] ... we’re meant to  leave  it.

[action] Cooper stares up at the rocket. He recognizes the
[action] arrangement of two CRAFT at the top.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Rangers.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] The last components of our one
[dialogue] versatile ship in orbit, the
[dialogue] Endurance. Our final expedition.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] What happened to the other
[dialogue] vehicles?

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] The Lazarus missions.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Sounds cheerful.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] Lazarus came back from the dead -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] He had to die in the first place.
[dialogue] You sent people out there looking
[dialogue] for a new home ...

[action] Professor Brand nods.
[] There’s no planet in our solar
[] system that can support life ...
[] and it’d take them a thousand years
[] to reach the nearest star - that
[] doesn’t even qualify as  futile  ...
[] Where did you send them, Professor?

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] Cooper, I can’t tell you any more
[dialogue] unless you agree to pilot this
[dialogue] craft. You’re the best we ever had.

[page_number] 31.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I barely left the stratosphere.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] This crew’s never left the
[dialogue] simulator . We can’t program this
[dialogue] mission from Earth, we don’t know
[dialogue] what’s out there. We need a pilot.
[dialogue] And this is the mission you were
[dialogue] trained for.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Without ever knowing. An hour ago,
[dialogue] you didn’t even know I was still
[dialogue] alive. And you were going anyway.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] We had no choice. But something
[dialogue] brought you here. They  chose  you.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Who’s ’they’?

[action] Professor Brand is silent. Cooper wrestles.
[] How long would I be gone?

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] Hard to know. Years.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I’ve got my kids, Professor.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] Get out there and save them.

[action] Cooper considers this. Decides.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Who’s ’they’?

[scene_heading] INT. CONFERENCE ROOM - LATER

[action] A man in his forties has the solar system up on the screen.
[action] This is ROMILLY.

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] We started detecting gravitational
[dialogue] anomalies almost fifty years ago.
[dialogue] Mostly small distortions to our
[dialogue] instruments in the upper atmosphere
[dialogue] - I believe you encountered one
[dialogue] yourself ...

[page_number] 32.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (realizing)
[dialogue] Over the Straights - my crash -
[dialogue] something tripped my fly-by wire -

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] Exactly. But the most significant
[dialogue] anomaly was this ...

[action] Cooper stares at an image of Saturn and its moons. Romilly
[action] zooms in on some stars DISTORTED like ripples in a pond.

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] A disturbance of spacetime out near
[dialogue] Saturn.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] A wormhole?

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] It appeared forty-eight years ago.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Where does it lead?

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] Another galaxy.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] A wormhole isn’t a naturally
[dialogue] occurring phenomenon.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Someone placed it there.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] ’They’.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] And whoever ’They’ are, they appear
[dialogue] to be looking out for us - that
[dialogue] wormhole lets us travel to other
[dialogue] stars. It came along right as we
[dialogue] needed it.

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] They’ve put potentially habitable
[dialogue] worlds within our reach. Twelve, in
[dialogue] fact from our initial probes.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You sent probes into it?

[page_number] 33.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] We sent  people  into it. Ten years
[dialogue] ago.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] The Lazarus missions.

[action] Professor Brand rises and moves to a MEMORIAL, pointing -

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] Twelve possible worlds. Twelve
[dialogue] Ranger launches carrying the
[dialogue] bravest humans ever to live, led by
[dialogue] the remarkable Dr Mann.

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] Each person’s landing pod had life
[dialogue] support for two years - but they
[dialogue] could use hibernation to stretch
[dialogue] that, making observations on
[dialogue] organics over a decade or more.
[dialogue] Their mission was to assess their
[dialogue] world, and if it showed promise,
[dialogue] send a signal, bed down for the
[dialogue] long nap, and wait to be rescued.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] And if their world didn’t show
[dialogue] promise?

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] Hence the bravery.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Because you don’t have resources to
[dialogue] visit all twelve.

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] No. Data transmission back through
[dialogue] the wormhole is rudimentary, simple
[dialogue] binary ’pings’ on an annual basis
[dialogue] to give some clue as to which
[dialogue] worlds have potential. One system
[dialogue] shows promise.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] One? Kind of a long shot.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] One system with  three  potential
[dialogue] worlds ... no long shot.

[page_number] 34.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] So if we find a new home, what
[dialogue] then?

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] That’s the long shot. There’s Plan
[dialogue] A and there’s Plan B. Did you
[dialogue] notice anything strange about the
[dialogue] launch chamber ...

[scene_heading] INT. LAUNCH FACILITY - MOMENTS LATER

[action] Cooper cocks his head, puzzling at the VAST chamber ...
[action] there are structures built SIDEWAYS around the CURVED
[action] walls...

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] This whole facility ... it’s a
[dialogue] vehicle? A space station?

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] Both. We’ve been working on it, and
[dialogue] others like it for twenty-five
[dialogue] years. Plan A.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] How does it get off the Earth?

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] Those first gravitational anomalies
[dialogue] changed everything - suddenly we
[dialogue] knew that harnessing gravity was
[dialogue] real. So I started working on the
[dialogue] theory - and we start building this
[dialogue] station.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] But you haven’t solved it, yet.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] That’s why there’s a Plan B.

[scene_heading] INT. LABORATORY - MOMENTS LATER

[action] TECHNICIANS work the complex, high-tech lab. Professor Brand
[action] and Cooper follow Brand to a large glass and steel
[action] apparatus.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] The problem is gravity. How to get
[dialogue] a viable amount of human life off
[dialogue] (MORE)

[page_number] 35.

[character] BRAND (cont’d)
[dialogue] this planet. This is one way - Plan
[dialogue] B. A population bomb. Almost five
[dialogue] thousand fertilized eggs, weighing
[dialogue] in at under 900 kilos.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] How could you raise them?

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] With equipment on board we incubate
[dialogue] the first ten. After that, with
[dialogue] surrogacy, the growth becomes
[dialogue] exponential - within thirty years
[dialogue] we might have a colony of hundreds.
[dialogue] The real difficulty of colonization
[dialogue] is genetic diversity,
[parenthetical] (Indicates vials.)
[dialogue] This takes care of that.

[action] Cooper looks at the equipment. Unenthusiastic.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] We just give up on the people here?

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] That’s why Plan A’s a lot more fun.

[scene_heading] INT. PROFESSOR BRAND’S OFFICE

[action] Professor Brand watches Cooper as he gazes over the vast
[action] tracts of ALGEBRA covering every available surface.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Where have you got to?

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] Almost there.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Almost? You’re asking me to hang
[dialogue] everything on an ’almost’?

[action] Professor Brand moves close to Cooper.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] I’m asking you to trust me.

[action] Cooper looks at the passion in Professor Brand’s eyes.

[page_number] 36.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] All those years of training - you
[dialogue] never told me.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] We can’t always be open about
[dialogue] everything, Coop, even if we want
[dialogue] to be. What can you tell your
[dialogue] children about this mission?

[action] Cooper considers this. Uneasy.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] Find us a new home. When you
[dialogue] return, I’ll have solved the
[dialogue] problem of gravity. You have my
[dialogue] word.

[scene_heading] EXT. FARMHOUSE - LATE DAY

[action] Donald, on the porch, gets to his feet as he sees Cooper’s
[action] pickup approaching. The truck pulls up. Murph TEARS past
[action] Grandpa into the house - Donald looks at Cooper,
[action] questioning.

[scene_heading] INT. HALL OUTSIDE MURPH’S ROOM - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper tries to open the door, but Murph has stacked a desk
[action] and chair against it -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Murph?

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Go! If you’re leaving, just go!

[scene_heading] EXT. PORCH - NIGHT

[action] Donald looks out at the night, taking it all in.

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] This world never was enough for
[dialogue] you, was it, Coop?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I’m not gonna lie to you, Donald -
[dialogue] heading out there is what I feel
[dialogue] born to do and it excites me. That
[dialogue] doesn’t make it wrong.

[action] Donald considers this. Turns to Cooper.

[page_number] 37.

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] It might. Don’t trust the right
[dialogue] thing done for the wrong reason.
[dialogue] The ’why’ of a thing? That’s the
[dialogue] foundation.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (sadly)
[dialogue] Well, the foundation’s solid.
[dialogue] (Gestures at landscape.)
[dialogue] We farmers sit here every year when
[dialogue] the rains fail and say ’next year’.
[dialogue] Next year ain’t gonna save us. Nor
[dialogue] the one after. This world’s a
[dialogue] treasure, Donald. But she’s been
[dialogue] telling us to leave for a while
[dialogue] now.
[dialogue] (Stares at the horizon.)
[dialogue] Mankind was born on Earth. It was
[dialogue] never meant to die here.

[action] Donald considers this. Scoops the dust off the rail.

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] Tom’ll be okay. But you have to
[dialogue] make it right with Murph.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I will.

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] Without making any promises you
[dialogue] don’t know you can keep.

[action] Cooper meets Donald’s gaze. Looks away, nodding.

[scene_heading] INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - MORNING

[action] Cooper’s hand, reaching in, removes the chair from the desk,
[action] barricading the door. Murph is lying on the bed, turned
[action] away. Cooper pushes the desk back gently. Enters, quietly.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You have to talk to me.

[action] Nothing.
[] I have to fix this before I go.

[action] Murph turns, tear-stained, angry cheeks blazing -

[page_number] 38.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Then I’ll keep it broken so you
[dialogue] have to stay.

[action] Cooper sits down on the bed next to Murph.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] After you kids came along, your
[dialogue] mother said something I didn’t
[dialogue] really understand - she said, ’I
[dialogue] look at the babies and I see myself
[dialogue] as they’ll remember me.’ She said,
[dialogue] ’It’s as if we don’t exist anymore,
[dialogue] like we’re ghosts, like now we’re
[dialogue] just there to be memories for our
[dialogue] kids.’ Now I realize - once we’re
[dialogue] parents, we’re just the ghosts of
[dialogue] our childrens’ futures.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] You said ghosts don’t exist.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] That’s right. I can’t be your ghost
[dialogue] right now - I need to exist.
[dialogue] Because they chose me. They chose
[dialogue] me, Murph. You saw it. Murph sits
[dialogue] up.

[action] Points at the shelves. The gaps.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] I figured out the message ...
[dialogue] (Opens her notebook.)
[dialogue] It was Morse code ...

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Murph -

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] One word. You know what it is?

[action] Cooper shakes his head sadly. Murph holds out her notebook -
[] ’STAY’. It says ’STAY’, Dad.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Oh, Murph.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] You don’t believe me?! Look the
[dialogue] books! Look at -

[action] Cooper takes his daughter in his arms ...

[page_number] 39.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] It’s okay, it’s okay ...

[action] Murph buries her head on Cooper’s shoulder, sobbing.
[] Murph, a father looks in his
[] child’s eyes and thinks - maybe
[] it’s them ... maybe my child will
[] save the world. And everyone, once
[] a child, wants to look into their
[] own dad’s eyes and know he saw how
[] they saved some little corner of
[] their world. But, usually, by then,
[] the father is gone.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Like you will be.

[action] Cooper looks at his daughter. Lies with head, not heart:

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] No. I’m coming back.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] When?

[action] Cooper reaches into his pocket. Pulls out two WATCHES.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] One for you. One for me.

[action] Murph takes the watch, curious. Cooper holds up his watch.
[] When I’m in hyper-sleep, or travel
[] near the speed of light, or near a
[] black hole, time will change for
[] me. It’ll run more slowly. When I
[] get back we’ll compare.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Time will run differently for us?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Yup. By the time I get back we
[dialogue] might even be the same age. You and
[dialogue] me. Imagine that ...

[action] Murph takes this in. Cooper sees he’s made a mistake.
[] Wait, Murph -

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] You have no idea when you’re coming
[dialogue] back.

[action] Cooper looks at his daughter.

[page_number] 40.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] No idea at all!

[action] Murph THROWS the watch - TURNS HER BACK.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Don’t make me leave like this.

[action] Nothing.
[] Please. I have to go now.

[action] Murph will not turn around. Cooper tries to rest his hand on
[action] the back of her head, but she shakes it off.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I love you, Murph. Forever. And I’m
[dialogue] coming back.

[action] Cooper walks slowly out. A BOOK DROPS FROM THE SHELF. Cooper
[action] turns to look at it. Then leaves.

[scene_heading] EXT. FARMHOUSE - MOMENTS LATER

[action] Cooper, mechanical, puts his small bag in the truck.

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] How’d it go?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Fine. It was fine.

[action] Cooper turns to Tom. Hugs him. Tight enough for both kids.
[] I love you, Tom.

[character] TOM
[dialogue] Travel safe, Dad.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (indicates farm)
[dialogue] Look after our place, you hear?

[character] TOM
[dialogue] Can I use your truck while you’re
[dialogue] gone?

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (smiles)
[dialogue] I’ll make sure they bring it back
[dialogue] for you.

[action] Cooper gets in. Starts the engine.
[] Mind my kids for me, Donald.

[page_number] 41.

[action] Donald nods. Cooper pulls out.

[scene_heading] INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS

[action] Murph jumps off the bed, GRABS the watch, RUNS downstairs.

[scene_heading] INT. PICKUP TRUCK - CONTINUOUS

[action] As Cooper drives he lifts the blanket in the wheel-well
[action] where Murph hid last time. Nothing. And we hear a
[action] COUNTDOWN...

[character] VOICE
[parenthetical] (O.S.)
[dialogue] TEN ... NINE ...

[scene_heading] EXT. FARMHOUSE - CONTINUOUS

[action] Murph RACES out of the house, watch in hand -

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Dad?! DAD?!

[character] VOICE
[parenthetical] (V.O.)
[dialogue] EIGHT ... SEVEN ...

[action] But Cooper is a dust trail far down the road.

[character] VOICE
[parenthetical] (O.S.)
[dialogue] SIX ... FIVE ...

[action] Murph SOBS as her grandpa puts his arms around her ...

[scene_heading] INT./EXT. PICKUP TRUCK ON DUSTY PLAIN - CONTINUOUS

[action] As Cooper drives away tears roll down his cheeks

[character] VOICE
[parenthetical] (O.S.)
[centered] FOUR ... THREE ... TWO ... ONE ...

[page_number] 42.

[scene_heading] INT. LAUNCH FACILITY - DAY

[character] VOICE
[parenthetical] (O.S.)
[dialogue] IGNITION.

[action] FIRE SHOOTS FROM THE BASE OF THE ROCKET ... The rocket RISES
[action] slowly from the pad, up into the sky ...

[scene_heading] INT. RANGER COCKPIT - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper, in his space helmet, lets the FORCE of the rocket
[action] vibrate through him ...

[character] VOICE
[parenthetical] (O.S.)
[dialogue] Stage one ... SEPARATION.

[action] Cooper starts to see the Earth’s curve through the window...

[character] VOICE
[parenthetical] (O.S.)
[dialogue] Stage two ... SEPARATION.

[action] And Cooper shakes loose the bonds of Earth.

[scene_heading] EXT. UPPER ATMOSPHERE - CONTINUOUS

[action] The rocket RIPS upwards into the sky.

[scene_heading] INT. RANGER COCKPIT - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper glances around the vibrating, cramped cockpit -
[action] Brand, Doyle, Romilly, Tars. Tars spots Cooper’s glance -

[character] TARS
[dialogue] All here, Mr Cooper. Plenty of
[dialogue] slaves for my robot colony.

[action] Cooper looks at him, confused.

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] They gave him a humor setting so
[dialogue] he’d fit in with his unit better.
[dialogue] He thinks it relaxes us.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] A massive, sarcastic robot. What a
[dialogue] great idea.

[page_number] 43.

[character] TARS
[dialogue] I have a cue light I can turn on
[dialogue] when I’m joking, if you like.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Probably help.

[character] TARS
[dialogue] You can use it to find your way
[dialogue] back to the ship after I blow you
[dialogue] out the airlock.

[action] Tars looks at Cooper. A beat. An LED turns on. Cooper shakes
[action] his head.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] What’s your humor setting, Tars?

[character] TARS
[dialogue] One hundred percent.

[action] Cooper turns to the instruments -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Take it to seventy-five, please.

[action] EARTH ORBIT - CONTINUOUS

[action] The Rangers streak across the Earth, settle into a low
[action] orbit.

[scene_heading] INT. RANGER COCKPIT - CONTINUOUS

[action] Quiet. Cooper stares down at the continents sliding by. He
[action] looks over at Brand who is doing the same, abstracted.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] We’ll get back.

[action] She stares at the land. The oceans.
[] It’s hard. Leaving everything. My
[] kids ... your father ...

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] We’re going to spend a lot of time
[dialogue] together ...

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (nods)
[dialogue] We should learn to talk.

[page_number] 44.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] And when not to.
[parenthetical] (Off look.)
[dialogue] Just trying to be honest.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Maybe you don’t need to be that
[dialogue] honest.
[parenthetical] (Turns to Tars.)
[dialogue] Tars, what’s your honesty
[dialogue] parameter?

[action] Tars DISENGAGES from the floor and MOVES to the rear airlock
[action] -

[character] TARS
[dialogue] Ninety percent.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Ninety? What kind of robot are you?

[character] TARS
[dialogue] Absolute honesty isn’t always the
[dialogue] most diplomatic, or safe form of
[dialogue] communication with emotional
[dialogue] beings.

[action] Cooper turns to Brand. Shrugs.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Ninety percent honesty it is, then.

[action] Brand looks at Cooper. Can’t help smiling.

[character] VOICE
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] Sixty seconds out...

[scene_heading] EXT. EARTH ORBIT - CONTINUOUS

[action] The Rangers approach a RING MODULE, fire retro-thrusters and
[action] slide gracefully into the center of the ring - the last
[action] piece of a large modular craft: the U.S.S. ENDURANCE. Four
[action] LANDERS (including the Rangers) are nestled inside the ring
[action] module.

[page_number] 45.

[scene_heading] INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - MOMENTS LATER

[action] Brand, Doyle and Romilly, following Tars, FLOAT through the
[action] cramped cabins, powering up. Tars powers up a second
[action] articulated machine, CASE.

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] Cooper, you should have control.

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, ENDURANCE (RANGER) - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper checks instruments -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Talking fine. Ready to spin?

[scene_heading] INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[action] Doyle and Romilly are strapped in - Brand grabs a handhold -

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[character] BRAND
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] All set.

[action] Cooper hits a switch ...

[scene_heading] EXT. EARTH ORBIT - CONTINUOUS

[action] Thrusters silently fire on the Endurance. It starts
[action] ROTATING.

[scene_heading] INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[action] The crew members settle in as gravity is established.
[action] Romilly is clearly struggling to find his sea legs in the
[action] rotating ship.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] You okay there?

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] Yup. Just need a little time -

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] There should be Dramamine in the
[dialogue] hab pod.

[page_number] 46.

[scene_heading] INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - LATER

[action] The crew listen to Professor Brand over the video link -

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[parenthetical] (on screen)
[dialogue] I miss you already. Amelia, be
[dialogue] safe. Give my regards to Dr Mann.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I will, Dad.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[parenthetical] (on screen)
[dialogue] Things look good for your
[dialogue] trajectory. We’re calculating two
[dialogue] years to Saturn.

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] That’s a lot of Dramamine ...

[action] Cooper thinks about two years. What it means to his kids.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (on screen)
[dialogue] Keep an eye on my family, sir.
[dialogue] Specially Murph. She’s a smart one.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[parenthetical] (on screen)
[dialogue] We’ll be waiting when you get back
[dialogue] ...

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, ENDURANCE - LATER

[action] Cooper and Doyle flick switches and check instruments -

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[parenthetical] (V.O.)
[dialogue] ... A little older. A little wiser.
[dialogue] But happy to see you ...

[scene_heading] INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[action] Brand, Romilly, Tars and Case strap in.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[parenthetical] (V.O.)
[dialogue] ’Do not go gentle into that good
[dialogue] night ...’

[page_number] 47.

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper turns to Doyle. HITS the thrusters.

[scene_heading] EXT. EARTH ORBIT - CONTINUOUS

[action] Endurance’s main engines FIRE. The craft PUSHES out of orbit
[action] -

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[parenthetical] (V.O.)
[dialogue] ’Rage, rage against the dying of
[dialogue] the light.’ God speed, Endurance.

[action] The craft accelerates away from Earth.

[scene_heading] INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - DAY

[action] The crew sets up their CRYO-BEDS. Cooper looks out at the
[action] diminishing Earth floating in the void. Brand joins him.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] So alone.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] We’ve got each other - Dr Mann had
[dialogue] it worse.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (points at Earth)
[dialogue] I meant them. Look at that perfect
[dialogue] planet. We’re not gonna find
[dialogue] another one like her.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] No. This isn’t like looking for a
[dialogue] new condo - the human race is going
[dialogue] to be adrift ... desperate for a
[dialogue] rock to cling to while they catch
[dialogue] their breaths. We have to find that
[dialogue] rock. Our three prospects are at
[dialogue] the edge of what might sustain
[dialogue] human life.

[action] Brand shows him a blurry image of a dark blue planet.
[] Laura Miller’s first. She started
[] our biology program.

[action] She shows him a red world, just a tiny dot.
[] And Wolf Edmunds is here.

[action] Cooper hears something in her voice.

[page_number] 48.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Who’s Edmunds?

[character] BRAND
[parenthetical] (fondly)
[dialogue] Wolf’s a particle physicist.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] None of them had family?

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] No attachments. My father insisted.
[dialogue] They knew the odds against ever
[dialogue] seeing another human being. I’m
[dialogue] hoping we surprise at least three
[dialogue] of them.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Tell me about Dr Mann.

[action] Brand replaces the screen image for a grainy, white orb.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Remarkable. The best of us. My
[dialogue] father’s protégé. He inspired
[dialogue] eleven people to follow him on the
[dialogue] loneliest journey in human history.
[dialogue] Scientists, explorers ... That’s
[dialogue] what I love - out there we face
[dialogue] great odds. Death. But not evil.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Nature can’t be evil?

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Formidable, frightening - not evil.
[dialogue] Is a tiger evil because it rips a
[dialogue] gazelle to pieces?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Just what we bring with us, then.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] This crew represents the best
[dialogue] aspects of humanity.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Even me?

[action] Brand looks at him. Smiles.

[page_number] 49.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Hey, we agreed, ninety percent.

[action] Brand moves to her cryo-bed. Cooper looks out at space.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Don’t stay up too late. We can’t
[dialogue] spare the resources.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Hey, I’ve been waiting a long time
[dialogue] to be up here -

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] You are literally wasting your
[dialogue] breath.

[action] Cooper nods at her. Joins Tars.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Show me the trajectory again.

[character] TARS
[dialogue] Eight months to Mars, then
[dialogue] counter-orbital slingshot around -

[action] Brand’s cryo-bed darkens.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (whisper)
[dialogue] Tars? Was Dr Brand -

[character] TARS
[dialogue] Why are you whispering? You can’t
[dialogue] wake them.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Were Dr Brand and Edmunds ...
[dialogue] close?

[character] TARS
[dialogue] I wouldn’t know.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Is that ninety percent, or ten
[dialogue] percent ’wouldn’t know’?

[character] TARS
[dialogue] I also have a discretion setting.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] So I gather ...
[parenthetical] (Rises.)
[dialogue] (MORE)

[page_number] 50.

[character] COOPER (cont’d)
[dialogue] But not a poker face.

[action] Tars watches Cooper head for the comm. station. He sits down
[action] to record a message. Awkward. Stuck. He dives in -
[dialogue] Hey, guys. I’m about settle down
[dialogue] for the long nap, so I figured I’d
[dialogue] send you an update ...

[scene_heading] EXT. OUTER SPACE - CONTINUOUS

[action] The Endurance slips away from the small blue planet ...

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (V.O.)
[dialogue] The Earth looks amazing from here
[dialogue] ... you can’t see any of the dust -

[scene_heading] EXT. CORNFIELDS - DAY

[action] A line of dust slides across the shimmering horizon.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (V.O.)
[dialogue] Hope you guys are doing great. This
[dialogue] should get to you okay ...

[scene_heading] EXT. FRONT PORCH, FARMHOUSE - DAY

[action] Donald watches two approaching vehicles kick up dust.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (V.O.)
[dialogue] Professor Brand said he’d make sure
[dialogue] of it. Guess I’ll say good night.

[action] Donald recognizes Cooper’s truck ... Murph BURSTS out of the
[action] house -

[character] MURPH
[parenthetical] (quiet)
[dialogue] Is it him?

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] I don’t think so, Murph.

[action] Donald rises to meet the truck. Professor Brand gets out.

[page_number] 51.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] You must be Donald. Hello, Murph.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Why’re you in my dad’s truck?

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] He wanted me to bring it for your
[dialogue] brother.

[action] Silence. Professor Brand reaches into his briefcase.
[] He sent you a message -

[action] Murph TURNS and goes back into the house. Donald takes a
[action] disc from Professor Brand.

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] Pretty upset with him for leaving.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] If you record messages, I’ll
[dialogue] transmit them to Cooper.

[action] Donald nods. Professor Brand looks up at the house.
[] Murph’s a bright spark. Maybe I
[] could fan the flame.

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] She’s already making fools of her
[dialogue] teachers. She should come make a
[dialogue] fool out of you.

[action] Professor Brand smiles. Donald looks up into the blue.
[] Where are they?

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] Heading towards Mars ...

[scene_heading] EXT. MARS - DAY

[action] The Endurance streaks away from Mars ...

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[parenthetical] (V.O.)
[dialogue] Next time we hear from Cooper,
[dialogue] they’ll be coming up on Saturn.

[page_number] 52.

[scene_heading] EXT. SATURN - DAY

[action] The Endurance settles into an orbit around the ringed giant.

[character] TOM
[parenthetical] (O.S.)
[dialogue] But they said I can start advanced
[dialogue] agriculture a year early ...

[scene_heading] INT. COMMUNICATIONS BOOTH, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper, blanket around his shoulders, watches a highly
[action] compressed video of Tom -

[character] TOM
[dialogue] Got to go, Dad. Hope you’re safe up
[dialogue] there.

[action] Tom makes way for Donald:

[character] DONALD
[dialogue] I’m sorry, Coop, I asked Murph to
[dialogue] say hi, but she’s stubborn as her
[dialogue] old man. I’ll try again next time,
[dialogue] stay safe.

[action] The video cuts out. Cooper gets up, puts a pair of EAR BUDS
[action] in his ears and heads into -

[subheading] OMITTED

[scene_heading] INT. HAB POD, RING MODULE - LATER

[action] Cooper enters. Romilly is staring out the window.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You good, Rom?

[action] Romilly looks at Cooper.

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] It gets to me, Coop. This tin can.
[dialogue] Radiation, vacuum outside -
[dialogue] everything wants us dead. We’re
[dialogue] just not supposed to be here.

[action] Cooper looks at him, sympathetic.

[page_number] 53.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] We’re explorers, Rom, on the
[dialogue] greatest ocean of all.

[action] Romilly bangs on the side of the ship.

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] Millimeters of aluminium. That’s
[dialogue] it. And nothing within millions of
[dialogue] miles that won’t kill us in
[dialogue] seconds.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] A lot of the finest solo yachtsmen
[dialogue] couldn’t swim. They knew if they
[dialogue] went overboard that was it, anyway.
[dialogue] This is no different.

[action] Romilly considers this. Cooper passes him his ear buds -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Here -

[action] And the sounds of a THUNDERSTORM wash over Romilly, sounds
[action] that take us to ...

[scene_heading] EXT. SPACE - CONTINUOUS

[action] The Endurance is a tiny speck before the ringed gassy giant.

[scene_heading] INT. NAVIGATION, RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper looks over Doyle’s shoulder - he’s flicking through
[action] images of star fields, distorted as if through a fish-eye
[action] lens.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] From the relay probe?

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] It was in orbit around the wormhole
[dialogue] - each time it swung around we got
[dialogue] images of the other side of the
[dialogue] foreign galaxy.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Like swinging a periscope around?

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] Exactly.

[page_number] 54.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] So we’ve got a pretty good idea
[dialogue] what we’re gonna find on the other
[dialogue] side?

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] Navigationally.

[action] Brand approaches.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] We’ll be coming up on the wormhole
[dialogue] in less than forty-five. Suit up.

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - LATER

[action] Cooper straps in, peering out at the inky blackness past
[action] Saturn. Romilly joins him there, excited.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] Strap in - I’m killing the spin ...

[scene_heading] EXT. SATURN - CONTINUOUS

[action] As the Endurance streaks past Saturn, it stops rotating,
[action] headed for a DISTORTED BLUR of stars.

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] There!

[action] He points at the SPHERICAL blur of stars.

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] That’s it! That’s the wormhole!

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Say it, don’t spray it, Rom.

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] Cooper, this is a portal, cutting
[dialogue] through spacetime -
[parenthetical] (Points.)
[dialogue] We’re seeing into the heart of a
[dialogue] galaxy so far away we don’t even
[dialogue] know where it is in the universe.

[action] Cooper stares at the wormhole as they approach: a massive
[action] spherical lens into another galaxy.

[page_number] 55.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] It’s a sphere.

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] Of course it is. You thought it
[dialogue] would be just a hole?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] No ... well, in all the
[dialogue] illustrations -

[action] Romilly grabs a piece of paper, draws two points, far apart
[action] -

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] In the illustrations they’re trying
[dialogue] to show you how it works -

[action] He pokes a hole in one point with his pen ...
[] So they say ’You wanna go from here
[] to there but it’s too far? A
[] wormhole bends space like this ...’

[action] He folds the paper over and jams the pen through the second
[action] point, connecting them.

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] ’So you can take a shortcut across
[dialogue] a higher dimension.’ But to show
[dialogue] that, they’ve turned
[dialogue] three-dimensional space ...
[parenthetical] (Gestures around.)
[dialogue] Into two dimensions.
[parenthetical] (Hold up paper.)
[dialogue] Which turns the wormhole into two
[dialogue] dimensions ... a circle.
[dialogue] (Indicates hole in paper.)
[dialogue] But what’s a circle in three
[dialogue] dimensions?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] A sphere.

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] Exactly.
[dialogue] (Points out window.)
[dialogue] It’s a spherical hole ...

[action] Cooper marvels at the concept. And at the looming sphere ...
[] And who put it here? Who do we
[] thank?

[page_number] 56.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I’m not thanking anyone till we get
[dialogue] through it in one piece.

[subheading] OMITTED

[scene_heading] EXT. WORMHOLE - CONTINUOUS

[action] As the Endurance SWINGS around the wormhole, the view of the
[action] foreign galaxy SWINGS in opposition, like an ENORMOUS
[action] SHAVING MIRROR ... it’s extremely disorienting.

[action] The Endurance fires retro-thrusters to slow, descending
[action] towards the wormhole ...

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper is at the controls. Doyle is next to him.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Any trick to this?

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] No one knows.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (glances at Doyle)
[dialogue] But the others made it, right?

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] At least some of them.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Thanks for the confidence boost.

[action] Cooper stares down into the vast lens of the wormhole.
[] Everybody ready to say goodbye to
[] our solar system?

[character] ROMILLY
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] To our galaxy ...

[action] Cooper pushes the sticks forward, nosing down and letting
[action] gravity PULL them towards the center of the wormhole ...

[page_number] 57.

[scene_heading] EXT. WORMHOLE - CONTINUOUS

[action] The Endurance reaches the surface of the wormhole. As it
[action] crosses the threshold it becomes apparent that THERE IS NO
[action] SURFACE ... the craft simply passes into the space of the
[action] distortion, its own warped reflection flickering towards it
[action] as if the ship were leaning into a giant shaving mirror ...

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper and Doyle stare at the distortion of space ahead ...

[scene_heading] INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[action] Brand and Romilly look out at reflections bordering the bulk
[action] - a space beyond our three dimensions ...

[scene_heading] EXT. WORMHOLE - CONTINUOUS

[action] The Endurance moves through a TUNNEL OF DISTORTED
[action] REFLECTIONS, seeming to gather more and more dizzying speed,
[action] but getting no closer to the far mouth, as if on an
[action] accelerating treadmill -

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper, awestruck, checks his instruments -

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] They won’t help you in here. We’re
[dialogue] cutting through the bulk - space
[dialogue] beyond our three dimensions ...
[dialogue] (Checks his equipment.)
[dialogue] All we can do is record and
[dialogue] observe.

[scene_heading] INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[action] Brand JUMPS - a shape in the air in front of her is BENDING,
[action] warping to form ripples in spacetime inside the cabin -
[action] Romilly STARES at the distortion -

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] What is that?!

[action] Brand watches the distortion move towards her -

[page_number] 58.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I think - I think it’s them.

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] Distorting spacetime? Don’t -!

[action] Brand is reaching out towards the warped space - it MOVES
[action] towards her, DISTORTING her hand - but Brand is not in pain
[action] ...

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper and Doyle watch the tunnel mouth STREAK towards them,
[action] a mass of stars and nebulae GROWING ...

[scene_heading] EXT. FAR SIDE OF THE WORMHOLE - CONTINUOUS

[action] The Endurance slides out of the wormhole.

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Suddenly, the instruments are chirping -

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] We’re ...  here .

[action] Cooper and Doyle look out at the new galaxy ...

[scene_heading] INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[action] Brand’s hand is back to normal. She stares at her fingers.

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] What was that?

[action] Brand flexes her fingers, delighted.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] The first handshake.

[scene_heading] EXT. FAR SIDE OF THE WORMHOLE - CONTINUOUS

[action] The cosmos is more CROWDED here - STAR upon STAR, NEBULAE
[action] ...

[page_number] 59.

[scene_heading] INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - LATER

[action] Doyle calls up data on a workstation -

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] The lost communications came
[dialogue] through -

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] How?

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] The relay on this side cached them.

[action] Doyle flicks through data -
[] Years of basic data - no real
[] surprises. Miller’s site has kept
[] pinging thumbs up, as has Mann ...
[] but Edmunds went down, three years
[] ago.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Transmitter failure?

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] Maybe. He was sending the thumbs up
[dialogue] right till it went dark.

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] Miller still looks good?

[action] Doyle nods. Romilly is drawing on a whiteboard -
[] She’s coming up fast ... with one
[] complication - the planet is much
