[] closer to Gargantua than we
[] thought.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Gargantua?

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] A very large black hole. Miller’s
[dialogue] and Dr Mann’s planets orbit it.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] And Miller’s is on the horizon?

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] A basketball around the hoop.
[dialogue] Landing there takes us dangerously
[dialogue] close. A black hole that big has a
[dialogue] huge gravitational pull.

[action] Cooper glances around the concerned faces -

[page_number] 60.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Look, I can swing around that
[dialogue] neutron star to decelerate -

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] It’s not that, it’s time. That
[dialogue] gravity will slow our clock
[dialogue] compared to Earth’s. Drastically.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] How bad?

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] Every hour we spend on that planet
[dialogue] will be maybe ... seven years back
[dialogue] on Earth.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Jesus -

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] That’s relativity, folks.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] We can’t drop down there without
[dialogue] considering the consequences.

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] Cooper, we have a mission - COOPER
[dialogue] That’s easy for you to say - you
[dialogue] don’t have anyone back on Earth
[dialogue] waiting for you, do you?

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] You have no idea what’s easy for
[dialogue] me.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Cooper’s right. We have to think of
[dialogue] time as a resource, just like
[dialogue] oxygen and food. Going down there
[dialogue] is going to cost us.

[action] Doyle steps up to the screen, points out three planets.

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] Look, Dr Mann’s data is promising,
[dialogue] but we won’t get there for months.
[dialogue] Edmunds is even further. Miller
[dialogue] hasn’t sent much, but what she has
[dialogue] sent is promising - water, organics
[dialogue] ...

[page_number] 61.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] You don’t find that every day.
[dialogue] DOYLE No, you do not. So think
[dialogue] about the resources it would take
[dialogue] to come back here ...

[action] They look at each other, considering. Cooper turns to
[action] Romilly -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] How far off the planet do we have
[dialogue] to stay to be out of the time
[dialogue] shift?

[action] Romilly indicates a spot on his white board.

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] Just back from the cusp.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] So we track a wider orbit of
[dialogue] Gargantua, parallel with Miller’s
[dialogue] planet but a little further out ...
[dialogue] take a Ranger down, grab Miller and
[dialogue] her samples, debrief and analyze
[dialogue] back here

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] That’ll work.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] No time for monkey business down
[dialogue] there - Tars, you’d better wait up
[dialogue] here. Who else?

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] If we’re talking about a couple
[dialogue] years - I’d use that time to work
[dialogue] on gravity - observations from the
[dialogue] wormhole. That’s gold to Professor
[dialogue] Brand.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Okay. Tars, factor an orbit of
[dialogue] Gargantua - minimal thrusting -
[dialogue] conserve fuel - but stay in range.

[character] TARS
[dialogue] Don’t worry, I wouldn’t leave you
[dialogue] behind ...
[parenthetical] (Swivels around.)
[dialogue] Dr Brand.

[action] She smiles at him.

[page_number] 62.

[scene_heading] EXT. BLACK HOLE, GARGANTUA - DAY

[action] A black sphere sucking light from the cosmos, visible by its
[action] distorting effect on the light of stars behind it - squeezed
[action] into a GLOWING, CURVED HORIZON. The Endurance approaches.

[scene_heading] INT. RANGER COCKPIT - DAY

[action] Cooper looks out at Gargantua. Doyle peers over his shoulder

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] A literal heart of darkness ...

[action] Brand points to a small glowing planet nearer the blackness.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] There’s Miller’s planet.

[action] Cooper turns to Case, the machine riding shotgun.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Ready?

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Yup.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Don’t say much, do you?

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Tars talks plenty for both of us.

[action] Cooper chuckles as he throws a final switch -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Detach -

[scene_heading] EXT. ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[action] The Ranger DETACHES from the ring module, like an X-1 from a
[action] B-29, FIRES retro-thrusters to slow and ... DROPS -

[scene_heading] EXT. THE BLACK HOLE, GARGANTUA - DAY

[action] The Ranger SHOOTS down towards Gargantua -

[page_number] 63.

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper is in awe at their acceleration -

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (into radio)
[dialogue] Romilly, you reading these forces?

[scene_heading] INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[action] Romilly studies data, marveling.

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] Unbelievable.
[dialogue] (Looks out at Gargantua.)
[dialogue] If we could see the collapsed star
[dialogue] inside, the singularity, we’d solve
[dialogue] gravity.

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper looks down at the eerie blackness sliding beneath -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] No way to get anything from it?

[character] ROMILLY
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] Nothing escapes that horizon. Not
[dialogue] even light. The answer’s there,
[dialogue] just no way to see it.

[scene_heading] EXT. GARGANTUA - CONTINUOUS

[action] The Ranger looks tiny as it STREAKS over the blackness, high
[action] above the GLOWING HORIZON. It is approaching Miller’s
[action] planet, a gleaming dark-blue world ...

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper studies his trajectory.

[character] CASE
[dialogue] This is fast for atmospheric entry.
[dialogue] Should we use the thrusters to
[dialogue] slow?

[page_number] 64.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] We’re gonna use the Ranger’s
[dialogue] aerodynamics to save the fuel.

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Air brake?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Wanna get in fast, don’t we?

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Brand, Doyle, get ready.

[scene_heading] EXT. MILLER’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS

[action] The Ranger STREAKS down towards the planet. It starts to
[action] encounter the STRATOSPHERE -

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS

[action] The craft starts to HOWL and SHAKE. Cooper studies the
[action] curving horizon, concentrating -

[character] CASE
[dialogue] We should ease -

[centered] COOPER
[dialogue] Hands where I can see them, Case!
[dialogue] Only time I ever went down was a
[dialogue] machine easing at the wrong moment
[dialogue] -

[character] CASE
[dialogue] A little caution -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Can get you killed, same as
[dialogue] reckless.

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] Cooper! Too damn fast!

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I got this.

[action] Cooper squeezes the shaking controls with white knuckles -

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Should I disable the feedback?

[page_number] 65.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] No! No, I need to feel the air ...

[scene_heading] EXT. STRATOSPHERE, MILLER’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS

[action] The Ranger glows WHITE HOT, slicing through FLAT CLOUDS -

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper peers out at the razor-like layers of cloud -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Do we have a fix on the beacon?

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Got it. Can you maneuver?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Gotta shave more speed. I’ll try
[dialogue] and spiral down on it -

[action] Doyle looks at Brand, nervous. She takes a breath.

[scene_heading] EXT. MILLER’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS

[action] The Ranger CUTS through cloud formations, BURSTING out into
[action] CLEARER AIR, HIGH ABOVE AN ENDLESS OCEAN -

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS

[action] The crew peer at SPARKLING WATER, streaking below them -

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] Just water.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] The stuff of life ...

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Twelve hundred meters out.

[action] Cooper BANKS sharply, eases down.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] It’s shallow. Feet deep ...

[page_number] 66.

[scene_heading] EXT. MILLER’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS

[action] The Ranger is low now, kicking up backwash -

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Seven hundred meters ...

[action] Cooper peers ahead -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Wait for it ...

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Five hundred meters ...

[action] Cooper YANKS the stick -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Fire!

[scene_heading] EXT. MILLER’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS

[action] The Ranger’s retro-rockets FIRE - killing the craft’s speed
[action] just feet from the surface. Water SPRAYS UP as the Ranger
[action] SLEWS diagonally, gear is lowered, the Ranger drops, its
[action] landing gear holding it just above the shallow water.

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Everyone BOUNCES with the impact. Then BREATHES.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Very graceful.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] No. But it was very efficient.
[parenthetical] (Looks at them.)
[dialogue] What’re you waiting for? Go!

[action] Brand and Doyle hurry out of their harnesses, helmets on.
[action] Case moves to the hatch. With a CRACK, the hatch opens and
[action] LIGHT and SPRAY whip inside ...

[page_number] 67.

[scene_heading] EXT. MILLER’S PLANET - MOMENTS LATER (DAY)

[action] Case climbs quickly from the craft, knee deep in the water.
[action] Brand and Doyle follow. Case TRACKS the beacon.

[character] CASE
[dialogue] This way, about two hundred meters.

[action] Brand and Doyle peer into the distance. Smooth, ankle-deep
[action] water to the horizon, where a distant MOUNTAIN RANGE LOOMS.
[action] They start splashing towards it in their heavy spacesuits
[action] ...

[character] DOYLE
[parenthetical] (panting)
[dialogue] The gravity’s punishing ...

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Floating through space too long?

[character] CASE
[dialogue] One hundred and thirty percent
[dialogue] Earth gravity.

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper listens to their chatter, IMPATIENT.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (under his breath)
[dialogue] Come on ...

[scene_heading] EXT. MILLER’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS

[action] Doyle falls behind. Brand pushes on. Ahead, Case stops.

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Should be here.

[action] Brand joins him, searching the shallows for some sign of
[action] Miller’s mission. She looks up, confused.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] If the signal’s coming from here -

[action] Case DROPS to his knees THRASHING under the water, like a
[action] bear fishing. Doyle arrives -

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] What’s he doing?

[page_number] 68.

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper notices something. In the distance. The mountains -

[scene_heading] EXT. MILLER’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS

[action] Case WRENCHES a piece of DAMAGED EQUIPMENT from the sea bed.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Her beacon ...

[action] Case starts lugging the beacon to the Ranger.

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] Wreckage. Where’s the rest ...?

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Towards the mountains!

[action] She starts moving fast towards some FLOATING OBJECTS.

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper is staring out at the horizon -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Those aren’t mountains ...

[scene_heading] EXT. MILLER’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS

[action] Brand pauses -

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] They’re waves -

[action] Brand looks closer - the ’mountains’ are moving, tiny lines
[action] of white sea spray are blowing from the tops ...

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper looks the other direction ... there is a MOUNTAIN
[action] WAVE BEARING DOWN ON THE SHIP ...

[page_number] 69.

[scene_heading] EXT. MILLER’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS

[action] Brand is searching the wreckage -.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] Brand, get back here!

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] We need the recorder -

[action] Doyle looks from Brand to Case, who is loading the beacon.
[action] Beyond him Doyle sees the mountain wave approaching -

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] Case, go get her!

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper hits the dash, frustrated.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Dammit! Brand, get back here!

[scene_heading] EXT. MILLER’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS

[action] Brand sloshes along, checking DEBRIS -

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] We can’t leave without her data -

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] You don’t have time!

[action] Case is back at Doyle.

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] Go, go!

[action] Case takes off towards Brand, who is trying to lift a piece
[action] of equipment from the water. She drops it and moves on -

[scene_heading] EXT. RANGER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper swings open the hatch, stands in the doorway, peering
[action] out at the approaching mountain waves - turns back to Brand
[action] -

[page_number] 70.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Get back here! Now!

[action] Brand has pulled something heavy from the wreckage - she
[action] SLIPS, the wreckage PINNING her down ... She looks back at
[action] the Ranger - sees the mountain wave THOUSANDS OF FEET HIGH
[action] ALMOST UPON THEM -

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Cooper, go! Go! I can’t make it!

[action] Cooper looks at Case RACING towards her -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Get up, Brand!

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] GO! GET OUT OF HERE!

[action] Case THROWS her onto his back and starts running. Doyle
[action] stands, mesmerized by the sheer liquid mountain face ...

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Doyle! Come on! Case has her!

[action] Doyle turns, starts sloshing back, the water RUNNING against
[action] his ankles now ... Two hundred yards behind, Case POUNDS
[action] through the shallows, Brand on his back -

[action] Cooper looks up at the EVER CLOSER MOUNTAIN WAVE - jumps
[action] inside.

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper powers up, as the wall of liquid fills his view.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Come on, come on ...

[action] He sees the water right upon them - RUNS back to the hatch.
[action] Doyle is at the foot of the ladder, Case RUNNING FAST.

[character] DOYLE
[dialogue] Go!

[action] Case JUMPS up the ladder, THROWS Brand inside - TURNS for
[action] Doyle. The Ranger TILTS, RISES - DOYLE IS RIPPED FROM CASE’S
[action] HAND - WATER RAGES ACROSS THE OPEN HATCH -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Shut the hatch!

[action] Case shuts the hatch. Cooper is throwing switches -

[page_number] 71.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Power down! Power down! We have to
[dialogue] ride it out!
[dialogue] (To Brand, furious.)
[dialogue] We are not prepared for this!

[scene_heading] EXT. MILLER’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS

[action] Doyle is DRAGGED under and away. The Ranger is SUCKED
[action] SIDEWAYS up the face of the mountain -

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Brand and Cooper are thrown across the cockpit. Case GRABS
[action] Brand, pulls her into her seat - Cooper holds on as the
[action] craft ROLLS and ROLLS -

[scene_heading] EXT. MILLER’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS

[action] The Ranger reaches the top of the wave, rocks upright -

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper drops into his seat as the water pours off the canopy
[action] -

[scene_heading] EXT. MILLER’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS

[action] The Ranger tilts over the backside of the wave, SURFING for
[action] a second then PITCHING FORWARD - TUMBLING DOWN 8,000 FEET
[action] ...

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS

[action] They hang on for dear life, THRASHED MERCILESSLY -

[scene_heading] EXT. MILLER’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS

[action] The Ranger comes AGROUND as the wave leaves it behind ...

[page_number] 72.

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS

[action] The craft comes to rest. Cooper jumps to the controls,
[action] powers up the electrics. The engines won’t respond -

[scene_heading] EXT. RANGER - CONTINUOUS

[action] The gear LIFTS the Ranger. Water FLOODS out -

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper tries the engines again. Nothing.

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Too waterlogged. Let it drain.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (hits console)
[dialogue] GODDAMN!

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I told you to leave me.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] And I told you to get your ass back
[dialogue] here! Difference is, only one of us
[dialogue] was thinking about the mission -

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Cooper, you were thinking about
[dialogue] getting home - I was trying to do
[dialogue] the right thing!

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Tell that to Doyle.

[action] Quiet. Cooper looks down at the clock. Bitter.
[] How long to drain, Case?

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Forty-five to an hour.

[action] Cooper shakes his head. Pulls his helmet off.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] The stuff of life, huh? What’s this
[dialogue] gonna cost us, Brand?

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] A lot. Decades.

[action] Cooper rubs his face. Mind reeling. Trying to breathe.

[page_number] 73.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] What happened to Miller?

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Judging by the wreckage, she was
[dialogue] broken up by a wave soon after
[dialogue] impact.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] How could the wreckage still be
[dialogue] together after all these years?

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Because of the time slippage. On
[dialogue] this planet’s time, she landed here
[dialogue] just hours ago. She might’ve only
[dialogue] died minutes ago.

[action] Case indicates the beacon.

[character] CASE
[dialogue] The data Doyle received was just
[dialogue] the initial status, echoing
[dialogue] endlessly.

[action] Cooper takes this in. Breathes hard. Takes off his gloves.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] We’re not prepared for this, Brand.
[dialogue] You’re a bunch of eggheads without
[dialogue] the survival skills of a boy-scout
[dialogue] troop.

[centered] BRAND
[dialogue] We got this far on our brains -
[dialogue] farther than any humans in history
[dialogue] -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Not far enough. And we’re stuck
[dialogue] here till there won’t be anyone
[dialogue] left on Earth to save -

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I’m counting every second, same as
[dialogue] you, Cooper.

[action] Cooper takes this in. They’re in the same boat.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Don’t you have some clever way we
[dialogue] jump into a black hole and get back
[dialogue] the years?

[page_number] 74.

[action] She shakes her head, dismissive.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Don’t just shake your head at me -!

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Time is relative - it can stretch
[dialogue] and squeeze - but it can’t run
[dialogue] backwards. The only thing that can
[dialogue] move across dimensions like time is
[dialogue] gravity.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (thinks)
[dialogue] The beings who led us here ... they
[dialogue] communicate through gravity ...

[action] Brand nods.
[] Could they be talking to us from
[] the future?

[character] BRAND
[parenthetical] (considers)
[dialogue] Maybe ...

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Well, if they can -

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Look, Cooper, they’re creatures of
[dialogue] at least five dimensions - to them
[dialogue] time may be just another physical
[dialogue] dimension. To  them  the past might
[dialogue] be a canyon they can climb into and
[dialogue] the future a mountain they can can
[dialogue] climb up ... but to us it’s not,
[dialogue] okay?

[action] Brand pulls her helmet off. Looks Cooper in the eyes.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I’m sorry, Cooper. I screwed up.
[dialogue] But you knew about relativity.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] My daughter was ten. I couldn’t
[dialogue] explain Einstein’s theories before
[dialogue] I left.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Could you tell her you were going
[dialogue] to save the world?

[page_number] 75.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] No. I wasn’t much of a parent, but
[dialogue] I understood the most important
[dialogue] thing - let your kids feel safe.
[dialogue] Which rules out telling a
[dialogue] ten-year-old that the world’s
[dialogue] ending.

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Cooper?

[action] Case is pointing out at another MOUNTAIN RANGE.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] How long for the engines?

[character] CASE
[dialogue] A minute or two -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] We don’t have it!

[action] The mountain wave is approaching. Cooper tries the engines -

[scene_heading] EXT. RANGER - CONTINUOUS

[action] The rockets COUGH and steam ...

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper tries the engines again. Close. But no ignition.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Helmets on!

[action] The wave is upon them ...

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Blow our cabin oxygen through the
[dialogue] main thrusters. We’ll spark it -

[action] Case hits a button - a HISS and SHRIEK of gas escaping ...
[action] Brand seals her helmet just as the cockpit DEPRESSURIZES -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Come on, now ...

[action] Cooper hits the engines - they BLAST TO LIFE -

[page_number] 76.

[scene_heading] EXT. RANGER - CONTINUOUS

[action] A fiery BLAST sends the Ranger clear of the mountain wave.

[action] Down below, Doyle’s body lies in the shallows, about to be
[action] swept up into the next rush of water ...

[scene_heading] INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - DAY

[action] Romilly watches as Cooper and Brand enter the Endurance.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Hello, Rom.

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] I’ve waited years.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] How many years?

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] By now ... it must be -

[character] TARS
[dialogue] Twenty-three years ...

[action] Cooper’s head lowers.

[character] TARS
[dialogue] ... four months, eight days.

[action] Cooper turns away.

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] Doyle?

[action] Brand’s eyes flicker down. She shakes her head. She grasps
[action] Romilly’s hands, looks up into his eyes, vulnerable -

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I thought I was prepared. I knew
[dialogue] all the theory. Reality’s
[dialogue] different.

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] And Miller?

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] There’s nothing here for us.

[action] Brand looks at Romilly’s wrinkles. His greying beard.

[page_number] 77.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Why didn’t you sleep?

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] I did a couple of stretches. But I
[dialogue] stopped believing you were coming
[dialogue] back, and something seems wrong
[dialogue] about dreaming your life away. I
[dialogue] learned what I could from studying
[dialogue] the black hole, but I couldn’t send
[dialogue] anything to your father. We’ve been
[dialogue] receiving, but nothing gets out.

[action] She looks up at Romilly, not wanting to ask ...

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Is he still alive?

[action] Romilly nods. Brand closes her eyes with relief.

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] We’ve got years of messages stored
[dialogue] ...

[action] Brand opens her eyes, looks for Cooper. He is in the comm.
[action] booth. He SHUTS the privacy curtain. She looks down.

[scene_heading] INT. COMMUNICATIONS BOOTH, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper studies the machine like it might bite him.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Cooper.

[character] COMPUTER VOICE
[dialogue] Messages span twenty-three years -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I know.
[parenthetical] (Whispers.)
[dialogue] Just start at the beginning.

[action] Cooper leans forward as the screen flickers to life: Tom,
[action] still seventeen, turns on the camera.

[character] TOM
[dialogue] Hi, Dad -

[action] Cooper pauses it. Prepares himself. Lets it run -

[page_number] 78.

[character] TOM
[dialogue] I met another girl, Dad. I really
[dialogue] think this is the one -

[action] Tom holds up a picture of himself and a teenage GIRL.
[] Murph stole Grandpa’s car. She
[] crashed it - she’s okay, though.
[] Your truck’s still running -
[] Grandpa said she should steal that
[] next time. I said if she did it’d
[] be the last thing she did ...

[action] Cooper leans back ...

[scene_heading] INT. COMMUNICATIONS BOOTH, ENDURANCE - DAY

[action] Cooper is holed up, still watching, unshaved. He’s been
[action] watching for days. On the screen, Tom is in his twenties -

[character] TOM
[dialogue] I’ve got a surprise for you, Dad.
[dialogue] You’re a grandpa ...

[action] Tom holds up an infant wrapped tight in swaddling.
[] Congratulations. Meet Jesse.

[action] Cooper smiles a tearful smile.
[] Grandpa said he already earned the
[] ’great’ part so we just leave it at
[] that.

[action] The screen cuts out. Then comes back on. Tom in his thirties
[action] -
[] Hi, Dad. I’m sorry it’s been
[] awhile. What with Jesse and all ...

[action] He stops, emotional.
[] Grandpa died last week. We buried
[] him out in the back forty, next to
[] Mom and Jesse.
[parenthetical] (Looks down.)
[] Where we’d have buried you, if
[] you’d ever come back.
[parenthetical] (Looks up.)
[] Murph was there for the funeral. I
[] don’t see her so much anymore.
[parenthetical] (Sighs.)
[] You’re not listening to this. I
[] know that. All these messages are
[] just out there, drifting in the
[] darkness ... I figured as long as
[] they were willing to send them
[] there was some hope, but ...

[page_number] 79.

[] (Pauses.)
[dialogue] You’re gone. You’re never coming
[dialogue] back. And I’ve known that for a
[dialogue] long time. Lois says - that’s my
[dialogue] wife, Dad - she says I have to let
[dialogue] you go. So I am.
[dialogue] (Reaches up to turn off
[dialogue] camera.)
[dialogue] Wherever you are, I hope you’re at
[dialogue] peace. Goodbye, Dad.

[action] The screen goes black. Tears are streaming down Cooper’s
[action] face. He stares at the black screen, wiping his face. He
[action] starts to get up - the screen flickers to life once more -

[action] A beautiful WOMAN of about forty has turned on the camera -
[action] she looks at us, unsure about this. Makes a start -

[character] WOMAN
[dialogue] Hello, Dad. You sonofabitch.

[action] Cooper peers into the face,  recognizing  -

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (whispers)
[dialogue] Murph?

[character] WOMAN (MURPH)
[dialogue] I never made one of these when you
[dialogue] were still responding cos I was so
[dialogue] mad at you for leaving. When you
[dialogue] went quiet, it seemed like I should
[dialogue] just live with my decision. And I
[dialogue] have ...
[parenthetical] (Looks around.)
[dialogue] But today’s my birthday. And it’s a
[dialogue] special one because you once told
[dialogue] me -

[action] She stops, unable to speak for a second.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] You once told me that when you came
[dialogue] back we might be the same age ...
[dialogue] and today I’m the age you were when
[dialogue] you left ...
[parenthetical] (Starts crying.)
[dialogue] So it’d be a real good time for you
[dialogue] to come back.

[action] Murph reaches up, switches off the camera and we stay with
[action] her in -

[page_number] 80.

[scene_heading] INT. COMMUNICATION ROOM, NASA - DAY

[action] Murph brings her hand down from the camera. Wipes her tears.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[parenthetical] (O.S., softly)
[dialogue] I didn’t mean to intrude.

[action] Murph turns to see Professor Brand, now ELDERLY, in a
[action] WHEELCHAIR in the doorway.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] I’ve never seen you in here before.

[action] Murph rises -

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] I’ve never been in here before.

[action] Murph wheels Professor Brand out into the corridor.

[scene_heading] INT. CURVING CORRIDOR, NASA - MOMENTS LATER

[action] Murph pushes Professor Brand.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] I talk to Amelia all the time. It
[dialogue] helps. I’m glad you’ve started -

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] I haven’t. I just had something I
[dialogue] wanted to get out.

[scene_heading] INT. PROFESSOR BRAND’S OFFICE - MOMENTS LATER

[action] Professor Brand wheels behind his desk.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] I know they’re still out there.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] I know.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] There are so many reasons their
[dialogue] communications might not be getting
[dialogue] through.

[character] MURPH
[parenthetical] (smiles gently)
[dialogue] I know, Professor.

[page_number] 81.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] I’m not sure which I’m more afraid
[dialogue] of ... they never come back, or
[dialogue] they come back to find we’ve
[dialogue] failed.

[action] She watches his introspection. Brings him back with -

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Then let’s succeed.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] (gestures at formula)
[dialogue] So, back from the fourth iteration,
[dialogue] let’s run it with a finite set.

[action] Murph has picked up a notebook. Pauses.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] With respect, Professor. We’ve
[dialogue] tried that hundreds of times.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] And it only has to work once,
[dialogue] Murph.

[action] She shrugs. Starts to work.

[scene_heading] INT. LAUNCH FACILITY - LATER

[action] Murph and Professor Brand sit, eating sandwiches on a
[action] walkway. WORKERS move about the CIRCULAR CHAMBER, building
[action] more SIDEWAYS INFRASTRUCTURE. Professor Brand looks down,
[action] proud.

[centered] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] Every rivet they drive in could
[dialogue] have been a bullet. We’ve done well
[dialogue] for the world, here. Whether or not
[dialogue] we crack the equation before I kick
[dialogue] -

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Don’t be morbid, Professor.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] I’m not afraid of death, Murph. I’m
[dialogue] an old physicist - I’m afraid of
[dialogue] time.

[page_number] 82.

[scene_heading] INT. PROFESSOR BRAND’S OFFICE - MOMENTS LATER

[action] Murph stands before the algebra. She REALIZES something.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Time ... you’re afraid of time ...

[action] CONVINCED, she TURNS -
[] Professor, the equation ...?

[action] He looks up.
[] For years we’ve tried to solve it
[] without changing the underlying
[] assumptions about time -

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] And?

[centered] MURPH
[dialogue] And that means each iteration
[dialogue] becomes an attempt to prove its own
[dialogue] proof - it’s recursive. Nonsensical
[dialogue] -

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[parenthetical] (sharp)
[dialogue] Are you calling my life’s work
[dialogue] ’nonsense’, Murph?

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] No, I’m saying you’ve been trying
[dialogue] to finish it with one arm - no,
[dialogue] with both arms tied behind your
[dialogue] back ...

[action] Murph focuses on Professor Brand, suddenly WARY ...
[] ... and I don’t understand why.

[action] Professor Brand looks down. Starts wheeling his chair.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] I’m an old man, Murph. Could we
[dialogue] pick this up some other time? I’d
[dialogue] like to go talk to my daughter.

[action] Murph nods. Looking at the Professor. Confused.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[parenthetical] (V.O.)
[dialogue] Stepping out into the universe, we
[dialogue] must first confront the reality
[dialogue] that nothing in our solar system
[dialogue] can help us ...

[page_number] 83.

[scene_heading] INT. COMMUNICATIONS BOOTH, ENDURANCE - NIGHT

[action] Brand watches her father on screen.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[parenthetical] (V.O.)
[dialogue] ... then we must confront the
[dialogue] realities of interstellar travel.
[dialogue] We must venture far beyond the
[dialogue] reach of our own life spans. We
[dialogue] must think not as individuals, but
[dialogue] as a species ...

[scene_heading] INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - DAY

[action] Romilly, Brand, Cooper, Tars and Case sit in discussion.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Tars kept the Endurance right where
[dialogue] we needed her, but it took years
[dialogue] longer than we anticipated ...

[action] Cooper puts both planets on screen - Dr Mann’s ice world,
[action] and Edmunds’ desert planet.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] We don’t have the fuel to visit
[dialogue] both prospects. We have to choose.

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] How? They’re both promising.
[dialogue] Edmunds’ data was better, but Dr
[dialogue] Mann is the one still transmitting.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] We’ve got no reason to suppose
[dialogue] Edmunds’ results would have soured.
[dialogue] His world has key elements to
[dialogue] sustain human life -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] As does Dr Mann’s.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Cooper, this is my field. And I
[dialogue] really believe Edmunds’ is the
[dialogue] better prospect.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (challenging)
[dialogue] Why?

[page_number] 84.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Gargantua, that’s why.
[parenthetical] (Steps to board.)
[dialogue] Look at Miller’s world -
[dialogue] hydrocarbons, organics, yes. But no
[dialogue] life. Sterile. We’ll find the same
[dialogue] thing on Dr Mann’s.

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] Because of the black hole?

[character] BRAND
[parenthetical] (nods)
[dialogue] Murphy’s Law - whatever can happen
[dialogue] will happen. Accident is the first
[dialogue] building block of evolution - but
[dialogue] when you’re orbiting a black hole
[dialogue] not enough can happen - it sucks in
[dialogue] asteroids and comets, random events
[dialogue] that would otherwise reach you. We
[dialogue] need to go to further afield.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You once referred to Dr Mann as the
[dialogue] ’best of us’.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] He’s remarkable. We’re only here
[dialogue] because of him.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] And he’s there on the ground
[dialogue] sending an unambiguous message that
[dialogue] we should go to that planet.

[action] Brand is silent. Romilly looks from Brand to Cooper.

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] Should we vote?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] If we’re going to vote, there’s
[dialogue] something you need to know. Brand?

[action] She says nothing.
[] He has a right to know.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] That has nothing to do with it.

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] What does?

[page_number] 85.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] She’s in love with Wolf Edmunds.

[character] ROMILLY
[parenthetical] (to Brand)
[dialogue] Is that true?

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Yes. And that makes me want to
[dialogue] follow my heart. But maybe we’ve
[dialogue] spent too long trying to figure all
[dialogue] this with theory -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You’re a scientist, Brand -

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I am. So listen to me when I tell
[dialogue] you that love isn’t something we
[dialogue] invented - it’s observable,
[dialogue] powerful. Why shouldn’t it mean
[dialogue] something?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] It means social utility - child
[dialogue] rearing, social bonding -

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] We love people who’ve died ...
[dialogue] where’s the social utility in that?
[dialogue] Maybe it means more - something we
[dialogue] can’t understand, yet. Maybe it’s
[dialogue] some evidence, some artifact of
[dialogue] higher dimensions that we can’t
[dialogue] consciously perceive. I’m drawn
[dialogue] across the universe to someone I
[dialogue] haven’t seen for a decade, who I
[dialogue] know is probably dead. Love is the
[dialogue] one thing we’re capable of
[dialogue] perceiving that transcends
[dialogue] dimensions of time and space. Maybe
[dialogue] we should trust that, even if we
[dialogue] can’t yet understand it.

[action] Brand looks at Romilly, who can’t meet her eye.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Cooper, yes - the tiniest
[dialogue] possibility of seeing Wolf again
[dialogue] excites me. But that doesn’t mean
[dialogue] I’m wrong.

[action] Cooper thinks back to his conversation with Donald.

[page_number] 86.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Honestly, Amelia ... it might.

[action] Romilly looks at Brand. It’s clear she’s lost.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Tars, set the course for Dr Mann.

[action] Brand is starting to tear up. She turns away.

[scene_heading] EXT. OUTER SPACE - MOMENTS LATER

[action] The thrusters FIRE, pushing the Endurance out of its orbit
[action] of Gargantua.

[scene_heading] INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - LATER

[action] Brand is checking her POPULATION BOMB. Cooper enters.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Brand, I’m sorry.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Why? You’re just being objective.
[parenthetical] (Beat.)
[dialogue] Unless you’re punishing me for
[dialogue] screwing up on Miller’s planet.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] This wasn’t a personal decision for
[dialogue] me.

[action] Brand turns from her equipment. Looks him in the eye.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Well, if you’re wrong, you’ll have
[dialogue] a very personal decision to make.
[parenthetical] (Off look.)
[dialogue] Your fuel calculations are based on
[dialogue] a return journey. Strike out on Dr
[dialogue] Mann’s planet, and we’ll have to
[dialogue] decide whether to return home, or
[dialogue] push on to Edmunds’ planet with
[dialogue] Plan B. Starting a colony could
[dialogue] save us from extinction.

[action] She closes the population bomb.
[] You might have to decide between
[] seeing your children again ... and
[] the future of the human race.
[parenthetical] (Smiles bitterly.)
[] (MORE)

[page_number] 87.

[character] BRAND (cont’d)
[dialogue] I trust you’ll be as objective,
[dialogue] then.

[scene_heading] EXT. OUTER SPACE - CONTINUOUS

[action] The Endurance sinks past a GLORIOUS NEBULA whose GOLDEN
[action] MISTS DISSOLVE TO ROILING BLACK CLOUDS and we are -

[scene_heading] EXT. FARMHOUSE - DAY

[action] Murph stands with Tom, now late forties. Watching a field
[action] BURN.

[character] TOM
[dialogue] We’ll lose about a third this
[dialogue] season. But  next year  ... I’m gonna
[dialogue] start working Nelson’s fields.
[dialogue] Should make it up.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] What happened to Nelson?

[action] Tom glances at her. Don’t ask. Heads for the house.

[scene_heading] INT./EXT. FARMHOUSE - DUSK

[action] Murph at family dinner with Tom, LOIS, and their
[action] six-year-old son COOP.

[character] LOIS
[dialogue] Will you stay the night? We left
[dialogue] your room like it was ...

[action] Murph looks down, awkward ...

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] No, I need to ...

[action] Murph looks upstairs. At Lois.
[dialogue] Too many memories, Lois.

[action] She nods. Coop helps Tom clear. As Coop takes Murph’s plate
[action] he starts COUGHING. Looks up at her, sees her concern,
[action] GRINS.

[character] COOP
[dialogue] The dust.

[action] He and Tom head into the kitchen.

[page_number] 88.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] I have a friend who should look at
[dialogue] his lungs, Lois.

[action] She nods, is about to speak. Tom sits back down.

[action] Pull back to reveal the glowing windows against the
[action] darkening plain, dust clouds rolling across the horizon ...

[scene_heading] INT. CORRIDOR, NASA - NIGHT

[action] Murph hurries down a corridor with a doctor, GETTY.

[centered] GETTY
[dialogue] He started asking for you after he
[dialogue] came to, but we couldn’t raise you
[dialogue] -

[scene_heading] INT. HOSPITAL ROOM, NASA - MOMENTS LATER

[action] Murph is at Professor Brand’s bedside. He is hooked up to
[action] machines. Barely breathing.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] Murph ... Murph ...

[action] Murph takes his hand with gentle concern.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] I’m here, Professor.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] I don’t have much life ...
[parenthetical] (Breathes.)
[dialogue] I have to tell you ...

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Try to take it easy.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] All these ... years. All these
[dialogue] people ... counted on me ...

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] It’s okay, Professor.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] I let you ... all you ... down.

[page_number] 89.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] No. I’ll finish what you started.

[action] Professor Brand looks up into Murph’s eyes, tears welling.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] Murph. Good, good Murph. Such faith
[dialogue] ... all these years, I told you to
[dialogue] have faith ... to believe ...

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] I do believe -

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] I needed you to believe your father
[dialogue] was coming back ...

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] I do, Professor -

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] Forgive me, Murph ...

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] There’s nothing to forgive.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] I  lied , Murph. I lied to you ...

[action] Murph looks at Professor Brand, confused.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] There’s no reason to come back ...
[dialogue] no way to help us ...

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] But Plan A - all this - all these
[dialogue] people ... the equation!

[action] But Professor Brand slowly shakes his head, tears rolling
[action] down. As Murph tries to comprehend, he settles, DRIFTING.

[character] MURPH
[parenthetical] (whispers)
[dialogue] Did he know?

[action] Nothing.
[] Did my dad know?!

[action] Nothing.
[] Did he abandon me?!

[page_number] 90.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] Do ... not ... go...

[action] She leans in to hear.

[character] PROFESSOR BRAND
[dialogue] Gentle ... into ... into ...

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] NO! NO! Professor, stay! You can’t!
[dialogue] You can’t leave ...

[action] Getty is at her shoulder.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] You can’t, you can’t, you ...

[action] Getty puts his hand on her shoulder. She sits there. Stuck.
[action] As Professor Brand goes still ...

[character] MURPH
[parenthetical] (V.O.)
[dialogue] Dr Brand, I’m sorry to tell you
[dialogue] that your father died today ...

[scene_heading] INT. COMMUNICATION ROOM, NASA - DAY

[action] Murph, controlled anger, sits in front of the camera.

[] MURPH
[] He had no pain and was ... at
[] peace. I’m sorry for your loss.

[action] She reaches to switch off the camera. STOPS. Acid.
[] Did you know, Brand? Did he tell
[] you ...?

[scene_heading] INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - DAY

[action] Murph’s voice rings through the empty ship. Only Case there
[action] to register it.

[character] MURPH
[parenthetical] (V.O.)
[dialogue] Did you know that Plan A was a sham
[dialogue] ...?! You left us here. To die.
[dialogue] Never coming back ...

[action] Through the window we see the Ranger moving away, towards -

[page_number] 91.

[scene_heading] EXT. DR MANN’S PLANET - DAY

[action] The Endurance orbits the silvery white globe as the Ranger
[action] heads towards the planet.

[scene_heading] EXT. STRATOSPHERE - MOMENTS LATER

[action] The Ranger drops through layers of large, MOUNTAINOUS CLOUD.

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper and Tars, Brand and Romilly. Cooper peers out,
[action] concerned, studying a heads-up display of CLOUD DENSITY ...

[scene_heading] EXT. DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS

[action] The Ranger cuts through one cloud, banks left and SCRAPES
[action] AGAINST THE NEXT ’CLOUD’, PANELS TEARING FROM THE WING - THE
[action] CLOUDS ARE ACTUALLY SOLID ICE FORMATIONS ...

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper banks away from the ICE, glancing out at the damage -

[scene_heading] EXT. DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS

[action] The Ranger moves cautiously through the ’cloudscape’ like a
[action] ship through an ice field ...

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Romilly and Brand put their helmets on. Tars indicates the
[action] beacon’s position. Cooper looks, BANKS the Ranger.

[scene_heading] EXT. DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS

[action] The Ranger’s gear lowers as it comes to rest, tentatively,
[action] at the base of what looks like a large CUMULUS CLOUD.

[scene_heading] EXT. DR MANN’S PLANET - LATER

[action] Cooper leads them up the ICE CLOUD. From a distance, they
[action] are walking on a cloud. Tars brings up the rear.

[action] Cooper crests a ridge. SPOTS something. He starts down
[action] towards a dirty orange dot in the cloudscape.

[page_number] 92.

[scene_heading] EXT. DR MANN’S POD - MOMENTS LATER

[action] Cooper is there. The others arrive at the large metal pod -
[action] WEATHERED and DAMAGED over the years, half buried in ice.
[action] Nearby, various WIRE MARKERS stick out of the ice. Tars
[action] starts digging out the hatch.

[scene_heading] INT. DR MANN’S POD - LATER

[action] A CRACK of COLD LIGHT, as the outer hatch is wrenched open.
[action] Cooper steps through the airlock, into a CRYPT-LIKE SPACE -

[action] Cooper’s hand sweeps ice from the nameplate of a
[action] cryo-chamber, ’DR MANN’.

[action] Tars fires up the cryo-chamber. The ice starts to melt.

[action] Cooper, helmet off, CRACKS the lid, pushes it back,
[action] revealing a figure in a plastic shroud. Cooper rips the seal
[action] ...

[action] Dr Mann’s eyes flicker open. He watches Cooper, breathing,
[action] focusing - reaches up with TREMBLING hands - GRABS Cooper -
[action] PULLS himself up, CHEEK AGAINST CHEEK - SOBBING - hands
[action] desperately CARESSING Cooper’s face. Cooper holds him tight.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (whispering)
[dialogue] It’s okay. It’s okay.

[scene_heading] INT. SAME - LATER

[action] Dr Mann sits, blanket over his shoulders, sipping from a
[action] steaming cup. He looks at their faces, marveling.

[character] DR MANN
[parenthetical] (cracked, parched)
[dialogue] Pray you never learn just how good
[dialogue] it can be to see another face.
[parenthetical] (Shaky sip.)
[dialogue] I hadn’t much hope to begin with.
[dialogue] After so much time, I had none. My
[dialogue] supplies were exhausted. The last
[dialogue] time I went to sleep, I set no
[dialogue] waking date.
[dialogue] (Looks at them all.)
[dialogue] You have literally raised me from
[dialogue] the dead.

[page_number] 93.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (smiles)
[dialogue] Lazarus.

[character] DR MANN
[parenthetical] (nods)
[dialogue] And the others?

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] I’m afraid you’re it, sir.

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] So far, surely?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] With our situation, there’s not
[dialogue] much hope of any other rescue.

[action] This hits Dr Mann hard. He looks down at his tea.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Dr Mann, tell us about your world.

[character] DR MANN
[parenthetical] (smiles gently)
[dialogue] My world. Yes. Our world, we hope.
[dialogue] Our world is cold, stark ...

[scene_heading] EXT. DR MANN’S PLANET - DAY

[action] Dr Mann leads the others up to the summit of a cloud.

[character] DR MANN
[parenthetical] (V.O.)
[dialogue] But undeniably beautiful ...

[action] From the top, they watch the planet’s pale sun setting.

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] The days are sixty-seven cold
[dialogue] hours, the nights are sixty-seven
[dialogue] far colder hours ...

[action] They make their way back into the shelter.

[character] DR MANN
[parenthetical] (V.O.)
[dialogue] The gravity is a very pleasant 80
[dialogue] percent of Earth’s. Up here, where
[dialogue] I landed, the ’water’ is alkali and
[dialogue] the ’air’ has too much ammonia in
[dialogue] it to breathe for more than a few
[dialogue] minutes ...

[page_number] 94.

[action] Brand checks readings on Dr Mann’s instruments.

[scene_heading] INT. DR MANN’S POD - NIGHT

[action] The crew are captivated by Dr Mann ...

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] But down at the surface, and there
[dialogue] is a surface ... the chlorine
[dialogue] dissipates and the ammonia gives
[dialogue] way to crystalline hydrocarbons and
[dialogue] breathable air. To organics.
[dialogue] Possibly even to life.
[parenthetical] (Off looks.)
[dialogue] Yes. We may be sharing this world.

[character] BRAND
[parenthetical] (giddy)
[dialogue] These readings are from the
[dialogue] surface?

[action] Brand is reviewing Dr Mann’s piles of data.

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] Over the years I’ve dropped various
[dialogue] probes.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] How far have you explored?

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] I’ve mounted several major
[dialogue] expeditions, but with oxygen in
[dialogue] limited supply, Kipp there had to
[dialogue] do most of the legwork.

[action] Dr Mann indicates a DEFUNCT ARTICULATED MACHINE.

[character] TARS
[dialogue] What’s wrong with him?

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] Degeneration. He misidentified the
[dialogue] first organics we found as ammonia
[dialogue] crystals. We struggled on for a
[dialogue] time, but ultimately, I
[dialogue] decommissioned him and used his
[dialogue] power source to keep the mission
[dialogue] going.
[parenthetical] (Remembers, sadly.)
[dialogue] I thought I was alone before I shut
[dialogue] him down.

[page_number] 95.

[character] TARS
[dialogue] Would you like me to look at him?

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] No, I think he needs a  human  touch.

[action] Tars turns to Brand.

[character] TARS
[dialogue] Dr Brand, Case is relaying a
[dialogue] message for you from the comm.
[dialogue] station.

[action] She nods.

[character] MURPH
[parenthetical] (V.O.)
[dialogue] ... He had no pain and was ... at
[dialogue] peace.

[scene_heading] INT. DR MANN’S POD - LATER

[action] Brand watches Murph’s message on Tars’ data screen.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] I’m sorry for your loss.

[action] Brand STARES. Cooper is there. Murph reaches up -

[character] BRAND
[parenthetical] (abstract)
[dialogue] Is that Murph?

[action] Cooper nods.
[] She’s become a -

[character] MURPH
[parenthetical] (acid)
[dialogue] Did you know, Brand? Did he tell
[dialogue] you? That Plan A was a sham ...?!

[action] Cooper looks at Brand, who is shocked.
[] You left us here to set up your
[] colony. Never coming back ...

[action] Murph does not want to ask, tears are running down her
[action] cheeks -
[parenthetical] (Small.)
[] Did my father know? Dad ...?

[action] Cooper stares. Murph’s eyes bore into his ...
[] Did you leave me here to die?

[page_number] 96.

[action] The screen goes dark. Cooper stands there, SHOCKED.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Cooper, my father devoted his whole
[dialogue] life to Plan A - I have no idea
[dialogue] what she means -

[character] DR MANN
[parenthetical] (O.S.)
[dialogue] I do.

[action] They turn. Dr Mann looks at them with gentle calm.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] He never even hoped to get people
[dialogue] off the Earth.

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] No.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] But he’s been trying to solve the
[dialogue] gravity equation for forty years!

[action] Dr Mann comes over, looks into Brand’s eyes.

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] Amelia, your father solved his
[dialogue] equation before I even left.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Then why wouldn’t he use it?!

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] The equation couldn’t reconcile
[dialogue] relativity with quantum mechanics.
[dialogue] You need more -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] More what?!

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] More data. You need to see inside a
[dialogue] black hole. And the laws of nature
[dialogue] prohibit a naked singularity.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (to Romilly)
[dialogue] Is that true?

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] If a black hole is an oyster, the
[dialogue] singularity is the pearl inside.
[dialogue] (MORE)

[page_number] 97.

[character] ROMILLY (cont’d)
[dialogue] Its gravity is so strong, it’s
[dialogue] always hidden in darkness, behind
[dialogue] the horizon. That’s why we call it
[dialogue] a black hole.

[centered] COOPER
[dialogue] If we could look  beyond  the horizon
[dialogue] -

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] Some things aren’t meant to be
[dialogue] known.
[parenthetical] (To Brand.)
[dialogue] Your father had to find another way
[dialogue] to save the human race from
[dialogue] extinction. Plan B. A colony.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Why not tell people? Why keep
[dialogue] building that damn station?

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] He knew how much harder it would be
[dialogue] for people to come together and
[dialogue] save the species, instead of
[dialogue] themselves ...
[dialogue] (To Cooper, sympathetic.)
[dialogue] Or their children.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Bullshit.

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] Would you have left if you hadn’t
[dialogue] believed you were trying to save
[dialogue] them? Evolution has yet to
[dialogue] transcend that simple barrier - we
[dialogue] can care deeply, selflessly for
[dialogue] people we know, but our empathy
[dialogue] rarely extends beyond our line of
[dialogue] sight.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] But the  lie . A  monstrous  lie ...

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] Unforgivable. And he knew it. Your
[dialogue] father was prepared to destroy his
[dialogue] own humanity to save our species.
[dialogue] He made the ultimate sacrifice.

[page_number] 98.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] No. That’s being made by the people
[dialogue] of Earth who’ll die because, in his
[dialogue] arrogance, he declared their case
[dialogue] hopeless.

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] I’m sorry, Cooper. Their case is
[dialogue] hopeless. We are the future.

[action] Cooper REELS. Brand puts her hand on his shoulder -

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Cooper, what can I do?

[action] He turns to her. Looks her in the eyes.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Let me go home.

[action] And the sound of WIND in DRY CORNSTALKS takes us -

[scene_heading] INT./EXT. PICKUP TRUCK ON DUSTY PLAIN - DAWN

[action] Murph drives. Getty next to her. In the distance, several
[action] fields BURN.

[character] GETTY
[dialogue] Are you sure?

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] His solution was correct. He’d had
[dialogue] it for years.

[character] GETTY
[dialogue] It’s worthless?

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] It’s half the answer.

[character] GETTY
[dialogue] How do you find the other half?

[action] Murph points at the sky.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Out there? A black hole. Stuck here
[dialogue] on Earth? I’m not sure you can.

[action] They pass vehicles PILED HIGH with belongings and people.

[page_number] 99.

[character] GETTY
[dialogue] They just pack up and leave. What
[dialogue] are they hoping to find?

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Survival.
[parenthetical] (Looks ahead.)
[dialogue] Dammit!

[action] A DUST STORM SWAMPS the truck, killing visibility. Murph
[action] pulls over. Kills the engine. The wind rocks the car.

[character] GETTY
[dialogue] Don’t people have a right to know?

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Panic won’t help. We have to keep
[dialogue] working, same as ever.

[character] GETTY
[dialogue] Isn’t that just what Professor
[dialogue] Brand ...?

[character] MURPH
[parenthetical] (sharp)
[dialogue] Brand gave up on us - I’m still
[dialogue] trying to solve this.

[character] GETTY
[dialogue] So you have an idea?

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] No. I have a ... feeling.

[action] Getty looks at her as she STARES out at the dust.

[action] INSERT CUT: MURPH (TEN), wet hair, towel around neck, turns
[action] and STARES at a book on the floor -

[character] MURPH
[parenthetical] (V.O.)
[dialogue] I told you about my ghost ...

[action] She stoops to pick up the toy next to it - a broken LUNAR
[action] LANDER.

[action] MURPH (FORTY) puts her hands on the glass, watching the sand
[action] scrape the car’s window ...

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] My dad thought I called it a ghost
[dialogue] because I was scared of it ...

[page_number] 100.

[action] INSERT CUT: MURPH (TEN) counts the books and gaps.
[action] MARVELING.

[character] MURPH
[parenthetical] (V.O.)
[dialogue] But I was never scared of it ...

[action] MURPH (TEN) takes out her notebook and starts drawing lines
[action] to represent the books.

[character] MURPH
[parenthetical] (V.O.)
[dialogue] I called it a ghost because it felt
[dialogue] like ...

[action] MURPH (FORTY) turns to Getty.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Like a person. Trying to tell me
[dialogue] something ...

[action] The storm is clearing. Murph starts the engine.
[] If there’s an answer here on Earth,
[] it’s back there, somehow. No one’s
[] coming to save us.
[parenthetical] (Pulls out.)
[] I have to find it ...

[action] Murph pulls past a pickup piled with worldly goods and
[action] people. She makes eye contact with two filthy kids in the
[action] back ...
[] And we’re running out of time.

[scene_heading] EXT. RANGER, DR MANN’S PLANET - DAWN

[action] Tars is up on the wing of the Ranger. Case pilots the LANDER
[action] in to land near the Ranger.

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper is sitting with his feet on the console.

[character] CASE
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] What about auxiliary oxygen
[dialogue] scrubbers?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] They can stay. I’ll sleep most of
[dialogue] the journey.
[parenthetical] (Wry.)
[dialogue] (MORE)

[page_number] 101.

[character] COOPER (cont’d)
[dialogue] I saw it all on the way out here.

[action] Romilly comes through the airlock. Removes his helmet.

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] I have a suggestion for your return
[dialogue] journey.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] What?

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] Have one last crack at the black
[dialogue] hole ...

[action] Tars enters.

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] Gargantua’s an older, spinning
[dialogue] black hole - what we call a gentle
[dialogue] singularity.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Gentle?

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] They’re hardly gentle, but their
[dialogue] tidal gravity is quick enough that
[dialogue] something crossing the horizon fast
[dialogue] might survive ... a probe, say.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] What happens to it after it
[dialogue] crosses?

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] Beyond the horizon is a complete
[dialogue] mystery - who’s to say there isn’t
[dialogue] some way the probe can glimpse the
[dialogue] singularity and relay the quantum
[dialogue] data? If he’s equipped to transmit
[dialogue] every form of energy that can pulse
[dialogue] - X-ray, visible light, radio -

[character] TARS
[dialogue] Just when did this probe become a
[dialogue] ’he’?

[action] Romilly looks from Tars to Cooper, sheepish.

[page_number] 102.

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] Tars is the obvious candidate. I’ve
[dialogue] already told him what to look for.

[character] TARS
[dialogue] I’d need to take the old optical
[dialogue] transmitter from Kipp.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (to Tars)
[dialogue] You’d do this for us?

[character] TARS
[dialogue] Before you get teary, try to
[dialogue] remember that as a robot I have to
[dialogue] do anything you say, anyway.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Your cue light’s broken.

[character] TARS
[dialogue] I’m not joking.

[action] Bing. The light flashes on.

[scene_heading] EXT. RANGER, DR MANN’S PLANET - DAY

[action] Brand and Dr Mann approach Cooper and Romilly at the foot of
[action] the ladder. Cooper addresses Brand -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Ranger’s almost ready. Case is on
[dialogue] his way back down with another
[dialogue] load.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I’ll start a final inventory.

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] Dr Mann, I need Tars to remove and
[dialogue] adapt some components from Kipp.

[action] Dr Mann considers this. Looks at Tars. At Romilly.

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] He musn’t disturb Kipp’s archival
[dialogue] functions.

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] I’ll supervise.

[action] Dr Mann considers this. Nods.

[page_number] 103.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] We need to pick out a site. You
[dialogue] don’t wanna move the module once we
[dialogue] land it.

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] I’ll show you the probe sites.

[action] Cooper glances around at the winds picking up -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Will conditions hold?

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] These squalls usually blow over.
[dialogue] You’ve got a long-range
[dialogue] transmitter?

[action] Cooper reaches up to check a box plugged in at his neck.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Good to go.

[action] Dr Mann points at a nozzle on his elbow -

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] Charged?

[action] Cooper checks, thumbs up. Dr Mann sets off, Cooper follows.

[scene_heading] EXT. DR MANN’S PLANET - LATER

[action] As Cooper follows Dr Mann down a ridge, they see the lander
[action] fly over. Cooper waves, reaches up to his long-range
[action] transmitter -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] A little caution, Case?

[character] CASE
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] Safety first, Cooper.

[action] Cooper follows Dr Mann down to the edge of the ice. They
[action] peer down a fifty-foot drop.

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] Just take it gently -

[action] He steps off, DROPPING - his ELBOW JETS FIRE, slowing his
[action] descent in time for him to land with a THUD. Cooper follows.

[action] They set out through a massive CANYON of ice ...

[page_number] 104.

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] Brand told me why you feel you have
[dialogue] to go back -

[action] Cooper STOPS.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] If this excursion is about trying
[dialogue] to change my mind, let’s turn
[dialogue] around right now.

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] No. I understand your position.

[action] He turns and starts walking. Cooper follows.

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] You have attachments. I’m not
[dialogue] supposed to, but even without
[dialogue] family, I can promise you that the
[dialogue] yearning to be with other people is
[dialogue] massively powerful. Our instincts,
[dialogue] our emotions, are at the
[dialogue] foundations of what makes us human.
[dialogue] They’re not to be taken lightly.

[action] The wind WHIPS ice crystals between them ...

[scene_heading] INT. KITCHEN, FARMHOUSE - DAY

[action] Murph introduces Getty to Lois and Coop. Getty pulls out a
[action] STETHOSCOPE and starts examining Coop, Murph slips upstairs
[action] ...

[scene_heading] INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - DAY

[action] Murph opens the door. Stands in her old bedroom. Feeling ...

[character] COOP
[parenthetical] (O.S.)
[dialogue] Mama lets me play in here ...

[action] Murph turns to see Coop. He points at a box on the shelf -
[] I don’t touch your stuff.

[page_number] 105.

[scene_heading] EXT. LANDER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Brand turns away as the lander touches down in a spray of
[action] ice.

[scene_heading] INT. DR MANN’S POD - CONTINUOUS

[action] Romilly watches Tars crouch down beside Kipp and connect
[action] Kipp to his own power. Kipp shows signs of life ...

[scene_heading] EXT. ICE CANYON, DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS

[action] Dr Mann waits for Cooper catch up. The WIND is picking up -

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] You know why we couldn’t just send
[dialogue] machines on these missions, Cooper?

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (breathless)
[dialogue] Frankly, no.

[character] DR MANN
[parenthetical] (pressing on)
[dialogue] A trip into the unknown requires
[dialogue] improvisation. Machines can’t
[dialogue] improvise well because you can’t
[dialogue] program a fear of death. The
[dialogue] survival instinct is our single
[dialogue] greatest source of inspiration.

[action] Dr Mann pauses to take a breath. Turns to Cooper.
[] Take you - a father. With a
[] survival instinct that extends to
[] your kids ...

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] That’s why I’m going home, hopeless
[dialogue] or not.

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] And what does research tell us is
[dialogue] the last thing you’ll see before
[dialogue] you die?
[parenthetical] (Off look.)
[dialogue] Your children. At the very moment
[dialogue] of death, your mind pushes you a
[dialogue] little harder to survive. For them.

[action] Dr Mann turns and starts walking out onto a massive ice
[action] field.

[page_number] 106.

[scene_heading] INT. KITCHEN, FARMHOUSE - DAY

[action] Murph brings Coop downstairs. Getty is listening to Lois’
[action] back. He looks up at Murph. GRAVE. Shakes his head ...

[character] GETTY
[dialogue] They can’t stay here.

[character] TOM
[parenthetical] (O.S.)
[dialogue] Murph?

[action] Murph turns to see Tom in the doorway.
[] What is this ...?

[scene_heading] EXT. LANDER, DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS

[action] Brand is counting flight cases when a WIND whips ice at her
[action] ...

[action] Kipp’s data screen. Confused.

[character] ROMILLY
[dialogue] I don’t understand.

[scene_heading] EXT. ICE FIELD, DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper and Dr Mann are like two ants on a sheet.

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] The first window’s up ahead -

[action] Cooper peers ahead to an OPENING in the ice. Dr Mann stops
[action] at the edge. Looks around the wind-blasted ice plane.
[] When I left Earth I felt fully
[] prepared to die. But I just never
[] faced the possibility that my
[] planet wouldn’t be the one. None of
[] this turned out the way it was
[] supposed to.

[action] Cooper peers over the edge at an enormous CREVASSE ...

[action] Dr Mann RIPS COOPER’S LONG-RANGE TRANSMITTER FROM HIS NECK -

[page_number] 107.

[action] He SLIPS backwards, but avoids going over the edge -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] What are you doing?!

[action] Until Dr Mann KICKS him. Cooper hits his jets, pushing
[action] himself onto a lower ledge of ice -

[scene_heading] INT. KITCHEN, FARMHOUSE - DAY

[action] Tom confronts Murph while Getty looks on -

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] They can’t stay here, Tom!

[character] GETTY
[dialogue] Not one more day -

[dialogue] Tom?!

[character] TOM

[dialogue] she’s done here.

[dialogue] -

[dialogue] Dad didn’t raise us, Grandpa did,
[dialogue] and he’s buried outside with Mom in
[dialogue] our ground. I’m not leaving them -

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] You have to, Tom -

[character] TOM
[dialogue] I’m a  farmer , Murph! You don’t give
[dialogue] up on the Earth.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] No! But she gave up on you! And
[dialogue] she’s poisoning your family.

[page_number] 108.

[scene_heading] EXT. DR MANN’S PLANET - DAY

[action] Cooper SCRAMBLES to his knees. Dr Mann approaches -

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] I’m sorry - I can’t let you leave.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Why?!

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] We’re going to need your ship to
[dialogue] continue the mission ... once the
[dialogue] others realize what this place
[dialogue] isn’t.

[action] Cooper’s mind races. He looks around.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You faked all the data?

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] I had a lot of time.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Is there even a surface?

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] I’m afraid not -

[action] Dr Mann KICKS Cooper over the edge, Cooper CLINGS on -

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] I tried to do my duty, Cooper, but
[dialogue] the day I arrived I could see this
[dialogue] place had nothing. I resisted the
[dialogue] temptation for years ... but I knew
[dialogue] there was a way to get rescued.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You fucking coward.

[action] Cooper BLASTS Dr Mann off his feet with his jet - SCRAMBLES
[action] up onto the ice. Dr Mann TACKLES him, they go down,
[action] WRESTLING. WIDER shows us two TINY FIGURES in a VAST
[action] LANDSCAPE, deciding the future of humanity with a BRAWL ...

[page_number] 109.

[scene_heading] INT. KITCHEN, FARMHOUSE - DAY

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Please, come with us.

[dialogue] comes back to save us all?

[dialogue] coming back. It’s up to us. To me.

[dialogue] couldn’t -

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] HE DIDN’T EVEN TRY!
[parenthetical] (Off look.)
[dialogue] He just abandoned us, Tom.

[character] MURPH

[character] TOM
[dialogue] Murph.

[dialogue] hands the box back to Coop.

[scene_heading] EXT. ICE FIELD, DR MANN’S PLANET - DAY

[action] Dr Mann LUNGES at Cooper, who SIDESTEPS him and PINS him ...

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Stop this!

[action] Dr Mann HEADBUTTS COOPER, FACEPLATE IMPACTS FACEPLATE.

[page_number] 110.

[action] AGAIN as he -

[character] DR MANN

[dialogue] WAY - FIRST -!

[character] COOPER

[action] faceplates have TINY FRACTURES ...

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] Best odds I’ve had in years -

[action] WHACK - He BUTTS Cooper’s faceplate, which CRACKS
[action] SICKENINGLY. AMMONIA HISSES INSIDE - COOPER ROLLS OFF, HANDS
[action] UP, DESPERATELY TRYING TO PLUG THE LARGE CRACK -

[action] Dr Mann RISES. Checks the fractures in his own helmet. Bends
[action] down to look at Cooper, STRUGGLING, CHOKING -

[character] DR MANN
[dialogue] Please don’t judge me, Cooper - you
[dialogue] were never tested like I was. Few
[dialogue] men have been ...

[action] Murph drives. Getty looks over, sympathetic.

[character] GETTY
[dialogue] You did your best, Murph.

[action] She just stares ahead at the road ...

[scene_heading] EXT. ICE FIELD, DR MANN’S PLANET - DAY

[character] DR MANN

[dialogue] survival instinct - that’s what
[dialogue] drove me. It’s always driven the
[dialogue] human race, and it’s going to save
[dialogue] it now. I’m going to save it. For
[dialogue] all mankind. For you, Cooper.

[page_number] 111.

[action] to talk to Cooper via the radio ...

[character] DR MANN

[dialogue] you ...

[action] TRANSMITTER -

[dialogue] children, yet?

[page_number] 112.

[page_number] 122.

[page_number] 123.

[scene_heading] EXT. ORBIT, DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
