[character] CASE
[dialogue] Endurance is hitting stratosphere -

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] She’s got no heat shield -!

[action] Cooper checks the lander’s speed against Endurance - pulls
[action] back on thrust as they come in below it -

[page_number] 124.

[scene_heading] EXT. STRATOSPHERE, DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS

[action] The lander is PERILOUSLY CLOSE to the RED HOT UNDERSIDE of
[action] the SPINNING ENDURANCE. The lander BANKS sideways, bringing
[action] its airlock within FEET of the spinning Endurance -

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper looks sideways at the spinning hull -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Case, you ready?

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Ready.

[action] Cooper watches the spinning hull, suddenly UNCERTAIN -

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Cooper?
[parenthetical] (Off look.)
[dialogue] This is no time for caution.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (grins)
[dialogue] If I black out, take the stick -
[dialogue] Tars, get ready to engage the
[dialogue] docking mechanism - Brand, hold
[dialogue] tight -

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Endurance is starting to heat -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] HIT IT!

[action] Case hits the RETRO-THRUSTERS. The view SPINS -

[scene_heading] EXT. STRATOSPHERE, DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS

[action] The lander goes into a FASTER AND FASTER SPIN as it, with
[action] Endurance, PLUMMETS towards the planet -

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS

[action] LIGHT FLASHES across their faces as the G-force of the spin
[action] PULLS THEM AGAINST THEIR RESTRAINTS. Cooper STRUGGLES to
[action] stay conscious - they BUFFET AGAINST THE ATMOSPHERE -

[action] Tars opens the airlock - the Endurance hatch above him is
[action] now SLOWLY ROTATING relative to him ...

[page_number] 125.

[scene_heading] EXT. STRATOSPHERE, DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS

[action] The GLOWING HOT Endurance and the lander PLUMMET, SPINNING
[action] towards the ice planet, whose curvature is FAST DISAPPEARING
[action] -

[scene_heading] INT. AIRLOCK, LANDER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Tars peers up as THE SPIN SPEEDS MATCH. He waits as the
[action] BUFFETING moves the hatches ... THEY LINE UP - he FIRES the
[action] GRAPPLES - they don’t connect - the hatches moved -

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Brand loses consciousness - Cooper watches the instruments,
[action] not the dizzying view, on the point of RED OUT -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Come on, Tars ... come on ...

[scene_heading] INT. AIRLOCK, LANDER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Tars sees the hatches roll back into ALIGNMENT. He FIRES
[action] AGAIN - this time THE GRAPPLES HOLD -

[character] TARS
[dialogue] GOT IT!

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper registers this. Case fires the retro-rockets to slow
[action] the spin.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Gen-- gentle, Case ...

[scene_heading] EXT. STRATOSPHERE, DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS

[action] The two craft, NOW JOINED, start to spin more SLOWLY ...

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper eases back into his seat as the G-force lessens -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Get ready to pull us up -

[action] The spin is slowing to almost nothing -

[page_number] 126.

[scene_heading] EXT. STRATOSPHERE, DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS

[action] Parts are RIPPING off the Endurance in the HEAT -

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper EASES into POWER on the main thrusters -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Come on. You can do it ...

[scene_heading] EXT. STRATOSPHERE, DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS

[action] The THRUSTERS on the lander start to DRAG both ships back up
[action] away from the planet, the heat DIMINISHING -

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper pulls back on the sticks, RELIEF washing over him.
[action] Brand comes to ... Cooper turns to Case, grinning -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Right? And for our next trick ...

[character] CASE
[dialogue] It’ll have to be good. We’re
[dialogue] heading into Gargantua’s pull -

[action] Cooper’s smile fades. He UNBUCKLES -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Take her -

[scene_heading] INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - MOMENTS LATER

[action] HISSING STEAM - RUSHING AIR - WHIRLING DEBRIS as Tars and
[action] Cooper (in suit and helmet) lock down different BULKHEADS -

[action] Brand (in suit and helmet) FLOATS alongside the POPULATION
[action] BOMB, checking the equipment -

[character] CASE
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] Cooper, we’re slipping towards
[dialogue] Gargantua - shall I use main
[dialogue] engines?

[page_number] 127.

[centered] COOPER
[dialogue] No! Let her slide as long as we can
[dialogue] -

[action] Cooper FLIES over to Tars, who is welding a bulkhead -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Give it to me.

[character] TARS
[dialogue] There’s good news and bad news -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I’ve heard that one, Tars - just
[dialogue] give it to me straight.

[scene_heading] INT. SAME - MOMENTS LATER

[action] Cooper SCRAMBLES to where Brand is checking her equipment.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] The navigation mainframe’s
[dialogue] destroyed and we don’t have enough
[dialogue] life support to make it back to
[dialogue] Earth. But we might scrape to
[dialogue] Edmunds’ planet.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] What about fuel?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Not enough. But I’ve got a plan -
[dialogue] let Gargantua suck us right to her
[dialogue] horizon - then a powered slingshot
[dialogue] around to launch us at Edmunds.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Manually?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] That’s what I’m here for. I’ll take
[dialogue] us just inside the critical orbit.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] And the time slippage?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Neither of us can afford to worry
[dialogue] about relativity right now.

[page_number] 128.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] I’m sorry, Cooper.

[action] They embrace, delicately touching faceplate to faceplate.

[scene_heading] EXT. ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[action] The CRIPPLED Endurance FALLS towards the HEART OF DARKNESS
[action] among the stars ...

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - DAY

[action] Cooper looks ahead at Gargantua. Preparing for battle.

[scene_heading] EXT. ENDURANCE - MOMENTS LATER

[action] The lander DETACHES, shifting its orientation ...

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] Once we’ve gathered enough speed
[dialogue] around Gargantua - we use the
[dialogue] lander 1 Ranger 2 as
[dialogue] rocket-boosters to push us out of
[dialogue] the black hole’s gravity ...

[action] The lander REATTACHES to the rear of the ring module.

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER 2 - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper slides into Ranger 2 - checking the systems.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] The linkages between landers are
[dialogue] destroyed ...

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Tars sits at the controls, running similar checks ...

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] So we’ll control manually. When
[dialogue] Lander 1’s spent, Tars will detach
[dialogue] ...

[page_number] 129.

[character] TARS
[dialogue] And get sucked into that black
[dialogue] hole.

[scene_heading] INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[action] Brand and Case listen to Cooper and Tars over the radio.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Why does he have to detach?

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] We have to shed mass if we’re gonna
[dialogue] escape that gravity.

[character] TARS
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] Newton’s third law - the only way
[dialogue] humans have ever figured out of
[dialogue] getting somewhere is to leave
[dialogue] something behind.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Cooper, you can’t ask Tars to do
[dialogue] this for us -

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER 2 - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper puts his hands on the controls -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] He’s a robot, Amelia - I don’t have
[dialogue] to ask him to do anything.

[scene_heading] INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[action] Brand is furious -

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] Cooper, you asshole -

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] Sorry, you broke up a little there.

[character] TARS
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] It’s what we intended, Dr Brand ...

[page_number] 130.

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Tars sits at the controls, ready.

[character] TARS
[dialogue] It’s our last chance to save people
[dialogue] on Earth - if I can find some way
[dialogue] to transmit the quantum data I’ll
[dialogue] find in there, they might still
[dialogue] make it.

[scene_heading] INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[action] Brand considers this.

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] If there’s someone still there to
[dialogue] receive it ...

[scene_heading] EXT. ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[action] The Endurance ACCELERATES towards the darkness ...

[scene_heading] INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[action] The black hole’s gravity makes the ship SHUDDER ... Brand,
[action] helmet on, tightens her harness ...

[scene_heading] EXT. ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[action] The Endurance STREAKS above the glowing horizon, SKIRTING
[action] the BLACKNESS beneath ...

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Tars looks out at the DARK OCEAN ...

[scene_heading] EXT. ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[action] The ship orbits the black hole with BLINDING ACCELERATION -

[page_number] 131.

[scene_heading] INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[action] The ship is SHAKING with GRAVITATIONAL ENERGY ...

[character] CASE
[dialogue] MAXIMUM VELOCITY ACHIEVED ...
[centered] PREPARE TO FIRE ESCAPE THRUSTERS -

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Tars checks his instruments -

[character] TARS
[dialogue] Ready.

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER 2 - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper checks his instruments -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Ready.

[scene_heading] INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[action] Brand looks out at the glowing horizon. Glances fearfully at
[action] the darkness below ... Case puts his hand on the button -

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Main engine ignition in three, two,
[dialogue] one, mark -

[action] Case hits the button -

[scene_heading] EXT. ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[action] The MAIN ENGINES FIRE - STRAINING AGAINST GARGANTUA ...

[scene_heading] INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[action] Brand feels the thrusters STRAIN to lift the craft -

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Lander 1 engines, on my mark,
[dialogue] three, two, one, mark -

[page_number] 132.

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS

[action] Tars hits the button -

[character] TARS
[dialogue] Fire.

[scene_heading] EXT. ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[action] Lander 1’s engines FIRE, adding to the thrust. The Endurance
[action] starts RISING away from the darkness ...

[character] CASE
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] Ranger 2’s engines, on my mark -
[dialogue] three, two, one, mark -

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER 2 - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper hits the button -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Fire.

[scene_heading] EXT. ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[action] Ranger 2’s engines add a fresh BLAST of fire, pushing the
[action] Endurance higher and higher ... back into the starlight ...

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER 2 - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper, shaking with the thrust, looks at his instruments -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] That little maneuver cost us
[dialogue] fifty-one years ...!

[scene_heading] INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[action] Brand holds on tight -

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] You don’t sound so bad for a
[dialogue] hundred and twenty!

[page_number] 133.

[scene_heading] EXT. ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[action] Lander 1’s engines DIE OUT ...

[character] CASE

[dialogue] Lander 1, prepare to detach, on my
[dialogue] mark ... three ...

[scene_heading] INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[action] Brand looks over at the lander -

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Two ...

[character] TARS
[dialogue] Detach.

[character] TARS
[parenthetical] (over radio)

[action] Something in this makes Brand frown, PUZZLED ...

[action] Lander 1 FALLS behind as the Endurance continues to RISE ...

[page_number] 134.

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER 2 - CONTINUOUS

[action] As Lander 1 FALLS back towards Gargantua, Ranger 2’s engines

[action] Case registers the burnout.

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Ranger 2, prepare to detach -

[action] Brand looks up, SHOCKED -

[character] BRAND
[dialogue] NO!

[action] She UNBUCKLES -

[character] CASE

[character] BRAND

[character] CASE
[dialogue] Three ...

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER 2 - CONTINUOUS

[character] COOPER

[dialogue] leave something behind.

[page_number] 135.

[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] Two ...

[scene_heading] INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[character] CASE
[dialogue] One ...

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER 2 - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper looks at her, fondly -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Hey, we agreed - ninety percent.

[character] CASE
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] Mark ...

[action] Cooper reaches for the button. Takes one last look at Brand
[action] - inside her helmet, Brand is crying, zero-G tears catching
[action] in her eyelashes like melted snowflakes.

[action] Cooper hits the button. But the word catches in his throat
[action] -.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Detach -

[scene_heading] EXT. ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[action] Ranger 2 DROPS AWAY from the Endurance ...

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER 2 - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper sees the Endurance ACCELERATE AWAY to a bright point
[action] as he FALLS AND FALLS ... Cooper starts to breathe FASTER -

[page_number] 136.

[scene_heading] EXT. GARGANTUA - CONTINUOUS

[action] Ranger 2 PLUMMETS towards blackness as the horizon GLOWS
[action] BRIGHTER and BRIGHTER - distorted starlight, plasma jets ...

[scene_heading] INT. COCKPIT, RANGER 2 - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper, trying to control his breathing, uses retro-rockets
[action] to TURN the lander down. He GASPS at the FLAMING HORIZON -

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] Tars? Are you there?

[action] STATIC - Ranger 2 TILTS down - INKY BLACKNESS ahead -

[scene_heading] INT. ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS

[action] Brand, crying, monitors Cooper’s lonely transmissions ...

[scene_heading] EXT. GARGANTUA - CONTINUOUS

[action] Ranger 2 PLUNGES towards the black hole. We hear Cooper’s
[action] panic breathing get LOUDER and LOUDER until -

[action] Ranger 2 SHUDDERS with EXPONENTIALLY RISING GRAVITATIONAL
[action] ENERGY as it CROSSES THE HORIZON - PLUNGING TOWARDS THE
[action] SINGULARITY - ALL WAVELENGTHS OF LIGHT CASCADING WITH HIM -

[action] AS WE -

[action] PLUNGE INTO ABSOLUTE ...

[action] WHITE -

[action] Not a whiteout - a SHIMMERING CAVALCADE OF ALL WAVELENGTHS:
[action] LIGHT, SOUND, EVERYTHING ... the SPHERICAL INSIDE OF THE
[action] BLACK HOLE, like a STAR turned INSIDE OUT. COOPER IS
[action] SCREAMING and we CUT to -

[action] BURNING CORN - men fighting a fire, Tom leading, GESTURING -

[scene_heading] INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - TWILIGHT

[action] Murph (forty) sits on the bed, looking into the BOX. She
[action] pulls out the LUNAR LANDER MODEL, looks up at the books ...

[page_number] 137.

[character] GETTY
[parenthetical] (O.S.)
[dialogue] Come on, Murph! We don’t have much
[dialogue] time!

[scene_heading] EXT. GARGANTUA - DAY

[action] A BLACK DOT appears, RUSHING TOWARDS US to become - A DARK
[action] SPHERE - we PLUNGE through it into SILENT DARKNESS - a WHITE
[action] SPHERE races towards us -

[action] Just as the wormhole was a spherical hole, THESE SPHERES ARE
[action] HOLES WITHIN HOLES ... we are dropping through A
[action] FOUR-DIMENSIONAL RABBIT HOLE -
[action] LIGHT/DARK/LIGHT/DARK/LIGHT/DARK with BLINDING RAPIDITY -
[action] the frequency almost SPEAKING. Cooper hangs on for dear life
[action] -

[character] COMPUTER VOICE
[parenthetical] (O.S.)
[dialogue] FUEL CELL OVERLOAD. DESTRUCTION
[dialogue] IMMINENT. INITIATE EJECTION.

[action] Cooper is LAUNCHED out of Ranger 2, which EXPLODES, and,
[action] PULLED to one side, MISSES A WHITE HOLE - PLUNGING INSTEAD
[action] TOWARDS A SMALLER GLASS-LIKE SPHERE -

[action] Cooper slows as he falls towards this sphere, reminiscent of
[action] the wormhole, but the light within is not stars but an
[action] infinity of WORLD LINES - (paths of objects through
[action] spacetime) -

[action] Cooper PLUNGES INTO THE WORLD SPHERE ... As he falls his
[action] SINGLE WORLD LINE stretches behind him - the INFINITE
[action] FUTURES OF HIS WORLD LINE splitting ahead to all the
[action] different possibilities in spacetime -

[action] Cooper himself is now like a ring being pulled down a cone
[action] of fabric. He STARES at the ORDERED CHAOS of world lines ...

[action] As he SLOWS his past and future world lines BREAK UP so they
[action] become like INFINITE REFLECTIONS IN PARALLEL MIRRORS ...

[action] Cooper’s world line DROPS into a SMALL, SQUARE TUNNEL -

[scene_heading] INT. THE TESSERACT - CONTINUOUS

[action] Tight enough to feel BLINDINGLY FAST at first, but Cooper
[dialogue] (and his INFINITE OTHERS) is actually SLOWING ... Cooper
[action] DESPERATELY reaches out, KNOCKING the sides of the tunnel,
[action] TRYING to slow himself - GRAPPLING - KICKING ’BRICKS’ out of

[page_number] 138.

[action] the ’walls’. He finally STOPS. Looks around in the SUDDEN
[action] CALM, FLOATING, catching his breath. He reaches out to the
[action] tunnel wall - CONFUSED -

[action] Each ’brick’ is TIGHTLY PACKED PAPER ... PAGES ... BOOKS -
[action] AS SEEN FROM BEHIND A SHELF ...

[action] Cooper PUSHES against a book - it MOVES SLIGHTLY. Cooper
[action] PUSHES, HARDER AND HARDER AND HARDER -

[action] The book drops out of sight, revealing -

[action] Murph, aged ten, wet hair, towel around her neck, TURNS,
[action] STARTLED by THE BOOK FALLING FROM HER SHELF.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Murph? Murph?

[action] She can’t hear him ...

[scene_heading] INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - MORNING

[action] Murph (ten) stands there, startled, STARING at the
[action] bookshelves. At the book on the floor, a broken toy beside
[action] it ...

[scene_heading] INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - TWILIGHT

[action] Murph (forty) looks at the bookshelves, REMEMBERING ...

[scene_heading] INT. THE TESSERACT - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper watches Murph (ten) cautiously approach - she
[action] CROUCHES. Picks something up -

[scene_heading] INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - TWILIGHT

[action] Murph (forty) turns the lunar lander in her hands. Thinking.

[scene_heading] INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - DAY

[action] Murph (ten) stands up holding the broken LUNAR LANDER ...

[page_number] 139.

[scene_heading] INT. THE TESSERACT - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper watches Murph (ten) examine the two pieces of the
[action] LUNAR LANDER MODEL -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] MURPH! MURPH!

[action] She turns ... leaves the room ... Cooper floats there,
[action] staring. He LOOKS around - each ’wall’ of the tesseract is a
[action] different view of Murph’s bedroom, so that by rotating he
[action] can effectively orbit her room ...

[action] He claws his way down to the next book wall. PUNCHES out two
[action] books -

[action] Murph’s bedroom, empty. The door opens, Cooper’s EARLIER
[action] SELF is standing there, staring at the room, perturbed.
[action] Murph (ten) joins Cooper, staring at the empty room ...

[action] Cooper LASHES out at the books - kicks a book out - SPOTS -

[action] Murph (ten) closes her door, crying, sliding the desk in
[action] front -

[scene_heading] INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - TWILIGHT

[action] Murph (forty) feels the desk. She puts her hand on the back
[action] of the chair, tilts it slightly, remembering -

[scene_heading] INT. THE TESSERACT - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper watches Murph (ten) put A CHAIR ON TOP OF THE DESK.
[action] The earlier Cooper nudges the door open -

[scene_heading] INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - EVENING

[action] Murph (ten) sees the door NUDGING against the desk and chair
[action] -

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Just go. If you’re leaving - just
[dialogue] leave now.

[page_number] 140.

[scene_heading] INT. THE TESSERACT - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper, the frustrated observer, spins around to see his
[action] EARLIER SELF nudging the door -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] (to his earlier self)
[dialogue] Don’t go, you idiot!

[action] His Earlier Self shuts the door ...

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Don’t leave your kids. You goddamn
[dialogue] fool.

[action] Cooper PUNCHES OUT books with all his strength -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] S ... T ...

[action] Murph (ten) is watching, no longer scared, fascinated -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] A ... Y ...

[action] Cooper STOPS. Catches his breath. Waits ...

[action] Earlier Cooper lifts the chair off the table to enter.

[action] Cooper watches his earlier self, FRUSTRATED -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Stay, you idiot! Tell him, Murph!
[dialogue] Stay ...

[action] As before, Cooper gives Murph the WATCH ... Murph THROWS THE
[action] WATCH and TURNS AWAY ...

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Murph, tell him again! Don’t let
[dialogue] him leave ...!

[scene_heading] INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - TWILIGHT

[action] Murph (forty) picks up the notebook. Opens it. Finds the
[action] word ’STAY’ ... looks up at the books, REALIZING ...

[page_number] 141.

[scene_heading] INT. THE TESSERACT - DAY

[action] Cooper is crying with frustration ...

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Murph, don’t let me leave ...

[action] Cooper watches as his earlier self heads to the door -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] STAY!!

[action] Cooper SMASHES a book from the shelf with all his might -

[action] His earlier self turns back. Looks at the book. Then leaves.

[action] Cooper rests his head against the books, SOBBING.

[scene_heading] INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - TWILIGHT

[action] Murph (forty) lowers her notebook, moves to the bookshelves,
[action] IN AWE ...

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Dad ... it was you. You were my
[dialogue] ghost ...

[action] She is crying. Joyful.

[scene_heading] INT. THE TESSERACT - DAY

[action] Cooper sobs at the back of the books. Lost ...

[character] TARS
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] Cooper?

[action] Cooper, STARTLED, turns. Tars is not there.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You survived.

[character] TARS
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] Somewhere. In their fifth
[dialogue] dimension. They saved us ...

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (frustrated)
[dialogue] Who’s ’They’? And why would they
[dialogue] help us?

[page_number] 142.

[character] TARS
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] I don’t know, but they constructed
[dialogue] this three-dimensional space inside
[dialogue] their five-dimensional reality to
[dialogue] allow you to understand it ...

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] It isn’t working -!

[character] TARS
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] Yes, it is. You’ve seen that time
[dialogue] is represented here as a physical
[dialogue] dimension - you even worked out
[dialogue] that you can exert a force across
[dialogue] spacetime -

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (realizing)
[dialogue] Gravity. To send a message ...

[action] Cooper looks around the infinite tunnel, infinite Coopers.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Gravity crosses the dimensions -
[dialogue] including time -

[action] Cooper THINKS ... He pulls himself up to a different wall,
[action] starts counting books ...
[] And you have the quantum data, now
[] -

[character] TARS
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] I’m transmitting it on all
[dialogue] wavelengths - but nothing’s getting
[dialogue] out ...

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I can do it -

[action] Cooper HITS a book’s world line, sending a WAVE ...

[character] TARS
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] Such complicated data ... to a
[dialogue] child ...

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Not just any child.

[page_number] 143.

[scene_heading] INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - TWILIGHT

[action] Murph (forty) stands there, looking at her old notebook -
[action] the page that says ’STAY’ ...

[character] GETTY
[parenthetical] (O.S.)
[dialogue] MURPH?! COME ON!

[action] She looks around the room, SEARCHING for an answer ...

[scene_heading] EXT. THE TESSERACT - DAY

[action] Cooper watches Murph (ten) looking out the window ...

[character] TARS
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] Even if you communicate it here,
[dialogue] she wouldn’t understand its
[dialogue] significance for years ...

[action] Cooper is seized by a sudden anger -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Then figure something out!
[dialogue] Everybody on Earth is going to die!

[character] TARS
[dialogue] Cooper, they didn’t bring us here
[dialogue] to change the past.

[action] Cooper hears something in this -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] We brought ourselves here ...

[action] Cooper PUSHES off, looking through the gaps in the books.
[action] Murph’s bedroom, full of DUST in the DUST STORM -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Tars, feed me the coordinates of
[dialogue] NASA in binary ...

[action] Cooper is in the room now, drawing a pattern in the dust ...

[action] Cooper watches Murph (ten) burst into the room. Murph stops
[action] and stares at the dust as Cooper’s Earlier Self comes in
[action] past her, SLAMS the window shut - sees the PATTERN of dust
[action] ...

[page_number] 144.

[scene_heading] INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - TWILIGHT

[action] Murph (forty) runs her finger along the DUST of the
[action] windowsill ... She turns to look around the room.
[action] Frustrated.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Come on, Dad. Is there something
[dialogue] else here ...?

[scene_heading] INT. TESSERACT - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper looks up from the floor -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Don’t you see, Tars? I brought
[dialogue] myself here. We’re here to
[dialogue] communicate with the
[dialogue] three-dimensional world. We’re the
[dialogue] bridge ...

[action] Cooper moves to another iteration of Murph’s room. Murph
[action] (ten) JUMPS up - GRABS the WATCH, RUNS out the door ...

[scene_heading] INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - TWILIGHT

[action] Murph (forty) looks at the watch, remembering. The second
[action] hand TWITCHES. She drops the watch back into the box ...

[scene_heading] INT. TESSERACT - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper pushes himself along the world line of the books ...

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I thought they chose me - they
[dialogue] never chose me - they chose Murph.

[character] TARS
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] For what?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] To save the world!

[action] Murph (ten) comes back into her bedroom, SOBBING. She is
[action] holding the watch. She puts it on the shelf.

[page_number] 145.

[scene_heading] INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - TWILIGHT

[action] Murph (forty) puts the box back on the shelf. SIGHS ...

[scene_heading] INT. TESSERACT - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper races FASTER and FASTER down the world lines.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] ’They’ have access to infinite
[dialogue] time, infinite space ...

[action] Cooper gestures at the INFINITIES in all directions ...

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] But no way to find what they need -
[dialogue] but I can find Murph and find a way
[dialogue] to tell her - like I found this
[dialogue] moment -

[character] TARS
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] How?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Love, Tars. Love - just like Brand
[dialogue] said - that’s how we find things
[dialogue] here.

[character] TARS
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] So what are we here to do?

[action] Cooper looks around the tesseract. The watch sits there on
[action] the shelf for as long as he can see ...

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] The watch. That’s it. She’ll come
[dialogue] back for it -

[character] TARS
[dialogue] How do you know?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Because I gave it to her. We use
[dialogue] the second hand. Translate the data
[dialogue] into Morse and feed it to me -

[action] Tars starts to transmit. Cooper GRABS the second-hand world
[action] line - starts to MANIPULATE it, sending waves down the world
[action] line ...

[page_number] 146.

[character] TARS
[dialogue] What if she never came back for it?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] She will. She will ... I  feel  it
[dialogue] ...

[action] The second hand is FLICKING back and forth ...

[scene_heading] INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - TWILIGHT

[action] Murph (forty) turns to leave ...

[character] GETTY
[parenthetical] (O.S.)
[dialogue] MURPH, HE’S COMING!

[action] She pauses. Goes back to the box - reaches in. PULLS OUT THE
[action] WATCH ... staring at it ... wondering ...

[character] GETTY
[parenthetical] (O.S.)
[dialogue] MURPH?! MURPH ...?!

[scene_heading] EXT. FARMHOUSE - TWILIGHT

[action] Getty is holding a tire iron, watching TOM’S TRUCK APPROACH.
[action] Lois and Coop STARE, FEARFUL, from the truck ...

[action] Tom APPROACHES, BLACK from soot ...

[action] Murph BURSTS out of the house ... right up to Tom ...

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Tom, he came back ... he came back
[dialogue] ...

[character] TOM
[dialogue] Who?

[action] She holds up the watch ...

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Dad. It was him. All this time ...
[dialogue] it was him. He’s going to save us
[dialogue] ...

[action] CLOSE ON the second hand of the watch, FLICKING back and
[action] forth -

[page_number] 147.

[scene_heading] INT. PROFESSOR BRAND’S OFFICE - DAY

[action] Murph furiously TRANSCRIBES the movements of the second hand
[action] -

[scene_heading] INT. CORRIDOR, NASA - LATER

[action] Murph, papers in hand, RUNS down the corridor, BUMPS into
[action] Getty - doesn’t stop ...

[scene_heading] INT. LAUNCH FACILITY - CONTINUOUS

[action] Murph runs to the railing of the catwalk above the enormous
[action] construction, looks down at the thousands of workers below.
[action] Getty comes out after her, curious. She looks at him, then
[action] SHOUTS OUT to the enormous space ...

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] EU-RE-KA!

[action] She turns to Getty - GRINNING.
[] Well, it’s traditional.

[action] She THROWS her paper out over the railing -
[] EUREKA!!

[action] Workers look up to see her papers flitting down ...

[scene_heading] EXT. THE TESSERACT - DAY

[action] Cooper looks out at the world line of the watch, of Murph,
[action] as it leads out into infinite complexity ...

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Did it work?

[character] TARS
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] I think it might have.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (hopeful)
[dialogue] Why?

[character] TARS
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] Because the bulk beings are closing
[dialogue] the tesseract ...

[page_number] 148.

[action] Cooper looks out to the distance - it is RAPIDLY
[action] APPROACHING, WORLD LINES BECOMING WORLD SHEETS, BECOMING
[action] BULKS ...

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Don’t you get it, yet, Tars? ’They’
[dialogue] aren’t ’ beings ’ ... they’re  us  ...
[dialogue] trying to help ... just like I
[dialogue] tried to help Murph ...

[centered] TARS
[parenthetical] (over radio)
[dialogue] People didn’t build this tesseract
[dialogue] -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Not yet ... but one day. Not you
[dialogue] and me but  people , people who’ve
[dialogue] evolved beyond the four dimensions
[dialogue] we know ...

[action] The tesseract EXPANSION BACK INTO FIVE DIMENSIONS IS ALMOST
[action] UPON THEM - Cooper BRACES HIMSELF -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] What happens now -?

[action] BAM - he is swept up in the expansion like a tiny leaf on a
[action] CHURNING WAVE -

[action] Cooper FLIES through the EXPANDING COSMOS, past PLANETS
[action] ORBITING STARS, WHICH BECOME ATOMIC PARTICLES, WHICH BECOME
[action] MATTER, BECOMING STARS ...

[action] Cooper APPROACHES A GLASSY TUBE. Inside is the OLD,
[action] UNDAMAGED ENDURANCE. As Cooper looks in from the bulk he
[action] sees: Brand, strapped in, Doyle opposite, traversing the
[action] wormhole for the first time ...

[action] Cooper REACHES for Brand ... She sees something, reaches up
[action] - their hands would touch if they weren’t in different
[action] dimensions, her fingers distorting the space of his fingers
[action] -

[action] WHAM! She, and the Endurance, are SWEPT PAST - Cooper is
[action] SMASHED into the spacetime of the wormhole - he SCREAMS AND
[action] WE -

[page_number] 149.

[scene_heading] INT. OUTER SPACE - LATER

[action] Cooper FLOATS, dead or unconscious, near Saturn. In the
[action] distance we see two Rangers approaching ...

[scene_heading] INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - DAY

[action] Cooper’s eyes flicker open. A bright room with an open
[action] window, net curtains obscuring the view. We hear the CRACK
[action] of a baseball off a bat. Children LAUGHING.

[character] VOICE
[parenthetical] (O.S.)
[dialogue] Mr Cooper? Mr Cooper?

[action] Cooper looks over to see a Nurse and a Doctor watching him.
[action] He tries to sit up.

[character] DOCTOR
[dialogue] Take it slow, sir. Remember you’re
[dialogue] no spring chicken any more.
[parenthetical] (Amused.)
[dialogue] I gather you’re one hundred and
[dialogue] twenty-four years old.
[dialogue] (Checks Cooper’s chart.)
[dialogue] You were extremely lucky. The
[dialogue] Rangers found you with only minutes
[dialogue] left in your oxygen supply -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Where am I?

[action] The Doctor, almost surprised, TURNS, moves to the window,
[action] opens the curtains. Where Cooper should see sky, he sees a
[action] CURVING UPSIDE-DOWN TOWN ...

[character] DOCTOR
[dialogue] Cooper Station. Currently orbiting
[dialogue] Saturn.

[action] The Nurse helps Cooper to the window. He looks out at the
[action] VAST CYLINDRICAL STATION - cornfields and buildings. Outside
[action] his window, kids are playing baseball. The batter hits a POP
[action] FLY ... the kids watch it up and up, until it carries on,
[action] falling up towards the buildings above. The kids SHOUT a
[action] warning - the ball smashes a skylight. Cooper watches the
[action] kids laugh.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Nice of you to name the place after
[dialogue] me.

[action] The Nurse giggles. The Doctor shoots her a look -

[page_number] 150.

[] What?

[character] DOCTOR
[dialogue] The station wasn’t named after you,
[dialogue] sir ... It was named after your
[dialogue] daughter.

[action] Cooper smiles at this ...

[character] DOCTOR
[dialogue] Although, she’s always maintained
[dialogue] just how important you were -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Is she ...
[parenthetical] (Braces.)
[dialogue] still alive?

[character] DOCTOR
[dialogue] She’ll be here in a couple weeks.
[dialogue] She’s really far too old for a
[dialogue] transfer from another station, but
[dialogue] when she heard you’d been found ...
[dialogue] well, this is Murphy Cooper we’re
[dialogue] talking about.

[character] COOPER
[parenthetical] (marveling)
[dialogue] Yes, it is ...

[action] The Doctor is wrapping up -

[character] DOCTOR
[dialogue] We’ll have you checked out in a
[dialogue] couple days.

[action] Cooper turns back to the window, thinking ...

[character] ADMINISTRATOR
[parenthetical] (V.O.)
[dialogue] I’m sure you’ll be excited to see
[dialogue] what’s in store ...

[scene_heading] INT. HANGAR, COOPER STATION - DAY

[action] The ADMINISTRATOR, thirties, leads Cooper along a walkway -

[character] ADMINISTRATOR
[dialogue] We’ve got a nice situation for you
[dialogue] ...

[page_number] 151.

[action] Cooper looks down at a line of SLEEK NEXT-GENERATION
[action] RANGERS. Sees a PILOT climb into one. Mechanics work another
[action] ...

[character] ADMINISTRATOR
[dialogue] I actually did a paper on you in
[dialogue] high school, sir. I know all about
[dialogue] your life on Earth ...

[scene_heading] EXT. TOWN SQUARE, COOPER STATION - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper looks at the strangely ordinary town the
[action] Administrator is walking him through ...

[character] ADMINISTRATOR
[dialogue] So when I made my suggestion to Ms
[dialogue] Cooper, I was delighted to hear
[dialogue] that she thought it was perfect ...

[scene_heading] EXT. FARMHOUSE - CONTINUOUS

[action] The Adminstrator leads Cooper through a cornfield ... The
[action] old farmhouse is there, preserved. As Cooper approaches, a
[action] small monitor starts playing the footage of OLD-TIMERS from
[action] the start of the movie.

[character] ADMINISTRATOR
[dialogue] Of course, I didn’t speak to her
[dialogue] personally ...

[action] As Cooper passes the monitor it changes to a FAMILIAR OLD
[action] LADY, but Cooper misses it ...

[scene_heading] INT. KITCHEN, FARMHOUSE - CONTINUOUS

[action] OLD-TIMERS play on video screens: a museum exhibit. The
[action] Administrator holds the door open for Cooper.

[character] ADMINISTRATOR
[dialogue] But she confirmed just how much you
[dialogue] loved farming.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] She did, huh?

[action] Cooper looks over the kitchen. Cooper sees a
[action] familiar-looking articulated machine -

[page_number] 152.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Is that ...?

[character] ADMINISTRATOR
[dialogue] The machine we found out near
[dialogue] Saturn when we found you, yes. Its
[dialogue] power source was shot, but we could
[dialogue] get you another if you want to try
[dialogue] and get it up and running again.

[action] Cooper turns to the Administrator.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Please.

[scene_heading] INT. HANGAR, COOPER STATION - EVENING

[action] Cooper, from the catwalk, watches the last of the Rangers
[action] come back from patrol. The PILOT jumps down as the ground
[action] crew wheels it into its place in the line of sleek ships.

[scene_heading] INT. KITCHEN, FARMHOUSE - NIGHT

[action] Cooper has Tars’ head laid out on the kitchen table.

[character] TARS
[dialogue] Settings: general settings,
[dialogue] security setting -

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Honesty. New level setting.
[dialogue] Ninety-five percent.

[character] TARS
[dialogue] Confirmed. Additional
[dialogue] customization?

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Yes. Humor, seventy-five percent.
[dialogue] Wait.
[parenthetical] (Thinks.)
[dialogue] Sixty percent.

[scene_heading] INT. HOSPITAL WAITING ROOM - DAY

[action] Cooper enters, nervous. A nurse is there.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Is she -?

[page_number] 153.

[character] NURSE
[dialogue] The family’s all in there.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] The family?

[character] NURSE
[dialogue] They all came along to see her -
[dialogue] she’s been in cryo-sleep for almost
[dialogue] two years.

[scene_heading] INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - CONTINUOUS

[action] Cooper cautiously pushes open the door. The bed is
[action] surrounded by people, grown-up children, grandchildren,
[action] babies ... They turn to look at him: some SMILES, some
[action] CURIOUS looks, a small child HIDES behind a parent’s leg ...

[action] Cooper approaches, and the family parts to let him see an
[action] ELDERLY WOMAN, lying in the bed, FRAIL.

[action] She looks up at Cooper. Delighted. Tears of joy. She reaches
[action] up to him ... he takes her hands.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Murph.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Dad.
[parenthetical] (To the others.)
[dialogue] Please.

[action] Her voice is a frail whisper. With authority. The family
[action] shuffles out. Cooper watches them go, turns back to Murph.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] You told them I like farming.

[action] Murph smiles, still mischievous. Cooper marvels at her.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Murph, it was me.  I  was your ghost.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] I know ...

[action] She lifts her wrist - the WATCH is there ...

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] People didn’t believe me, they
[dialogue] thought I’d done it all myself ...
[parenthetical] (Taps watch.)
[dialogue] (MORE)

[page_number] 154.

[character] MURPH (cont’d)
[dialogue] But I knew who it was ...

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] A father looks in his child’s eyes
[dialogue] and thinks - maybe it’s them -
[dialogue] maybe my child will save the world.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] And everyone, once a child, wants
[dialogue] to looks into their own dad’s eyes
[dialogue] and know he saw. But, usually, by
[dialogue] then, the father is gone. Nobody
[dialogue] believed me, but I knew you’d come
[dialogue] back.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] How?

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] Because my dad promised me.

[action] Cooper is crying now.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] I’m here now. I’m here for you,
[dialogue] Murph.

[action] Murph is shaking her head.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] No parent should have to watch
[dialogue] their child die. My kids are here
[dialogue] for me now. Go.

[character] COOPER
[dialogue] Where?

[character] MURPH
[parenthetical] (it’s so obvious)
[dialogue] Brand.

[action] And the family comes back in as Cooper releases Murph’s
[action] hand, stepping back to let Murph’s kids and grandkids swarm
[action] over her ... He watches them, their love, as if from another
[action] dimension. A man out of time. A ghost.

[character] MURPH
[parenthetical] (V.O.)
[dialogue] She’s out there ...

[page_number] 155.

[scene_heading] EXT. EDMUNDS’ DESERT PLANET - DAY

[action] Brand, in suit and helmet, stands watching Case excavate a
[action] pod, buried under a massive rock fall. She is crying.

[character] MURPH
[parenthetical] (V.O.)
[dialogue] Setting up camp ...

[scene_heading] EXT. HANGAR, COOPER STATION - NIGHT

[action] MECHANIC finishes looking over one of the sleek new Rangers.
[action] He packs his tools and heads out.

[action] A figure unfolds itself in the shadows - Tars. He picks his
[action] way through the shadows, unlocks the door. Cooper DARTS in
[action] ...

[scene_heading] EXT. EDMUNDS’ DESERT PLANET - DAY

[action] Brand kneels in front of a small CROSS. Edmunds’ nameplate
[action] hangs from it.

[character] MURPH
[dialogue] ... Alone in a strange galaxy ...

[action] She unseals her helmet - PULLS IT OFF ...

[scene_heading] INT. HANGAR, COOPER STATION - NIGHT

[action] Cooper and Tars scurry down the line of sleek ships. Cooper
[action] points - Tars starts working the hatch mechanism, while
[action] Cooper KEEPS WATCH ...

[scene_heading] EXT. EDMUNDS’ DESERT PLANET - DAY

[action] Brand, helmet off, BREATHES. And breathes again.

[character] MURPH
[parenthetical] (V.O.)
[dialogue] Maybe, right now, she’s settling in
[dialogue] for the long nap ...

[page_number] 156.

[scene_heading] INT. RANGER IN HANGAR, COOPER STATION - NIGHT

[action] Cooper STRAPS into the pilot’s chair, Tars beside him. The
[action] outer doors slide open. They look out at the inky blackness
[action] of space ...

[scene_heading] EXT. EDMUNDS’ DESERT PLANET - DUSK

[action] Brand looks at the setting sun ...

[character] MURPH
[parenthetical] (V.O.)
[dialogue] By the light of our new sun ...

[scene_heading] INT. HANGAR, COOPER STATION - MORNING

[action] The Mechanic opening up, walks along the row of ships until
[action] -

[action] One is MISSING.

[scene_heading] EXT. EDMUNDS’ DESERT PLANET - DUSK

[action] Brand turns from the dwindling light ...

[character] MURPH
[parenthetical] (V.O.)
[dialogue] In our new home.

[action] She heads down through the twilight towards camp. And we -

[transition] Fade out.

[action] Credits.

[action] End.
